system_overview:
The DCDC converter is responsible for converting the energy between 800V HV Bus of the ESS and 400V HV Bus of the IPU.
400V Power Conversion (Buck) – The HV DCDC shall convert electrical power from the 800V HV bus to the 400V HV bus. The HV DCDC shall supply power to the 400V bus in form of electrical current and maintain the voltage at the 400V bus to sustain the loads on 12V HVDCDC, PTC and eAC.
800V Power Conversion (Boost) – The DCDC shall convert electrical power from the 400V HV bus to the 800V ESS HV bus during charging. The DCDC shall control voltage and current on the 800V bus during charging.
Control Topology – The HV DCDC shall respond to commands from the Electric Drive Supervisory Unit for all supported functions.
Cooling – The HV DCDC shall have one inlet/outlet cooling interfaces to cool the internal components.
Figure 1: System Input/Output Diagram
Figure 2: System Architecture Diagram
scope:
The software block diagram is a logical view of the software being specified with a set of logical blocks and each block has a set of requirements.
Application Business Logic – This module contains the handling of overall application state machine, start-up and runtime routines and diagnostic, communication glue logics.
Middle Ware Communication – This module contains the CAN Communication logic for vehicle communication.
Middle Ware Diagnostics – This module contains the Diagnostics logic for failures monitoring.
Middle Ware Sensors – This module contains the Sensor interfacing logic for Current, Voltage and temperature sensors.
Middle Ware PMIC IF – This module contains the Diagnostics logic for failures monitoring.
Middle Ware Scheduler – This module contains the task scheduler.
HAL – This module contains hardware abstraction layer for MCU peripherals.
MCU– Infineon SAK-TC397XA-256F300W is selected as choice of used in this project.
Figure 2: Logical View
purpose: The purpose of the document is to specify software requirements for the P18 HV DCDC Project.

safety_requirements.logical_block: Fault levels to the vehicle CAN network for defining the faults and its reaction on CAN.
safety_requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall have fault level categorization, diagnostic debounces, fault reactions on HVDCDC system and the vehicle, and the CAN interface for HVDCDC to report the fault level to the vehicle CAN network.
safety_requirements.inputs: The fault levels to the vehicle CAN network for defining the faults and its reaction on HVDCDC system Or Vehicle.
safety_requirements.process:
At No Fault:
Fault Severity: NA
Fault categorization guide: NA
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X0 "No Fault"
Debounce strategy: NA
HVDCDC Fault Reaction: Normal Operation
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: NA
Set DTC in HVDCDC: NA
Vehicle reaction: Normal Operation
At Level 0:
Fault Severity: LOW
Fault categorization guide:
1) The failure effects are invisible to rest of HV system / driver; and
2) HVDCDC or vehicle do not have to alternate its performance to mitigate the failure.
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X1 " Level 0"
Debounce strategy: HVDCDC shall apply appropriate debounce strategy (e.g. counter-based or timer-based) to confirm the fault. This is required to prevent fault toggling or serve system rection on false diagnostic, for robustness.
HVDCDC Fault Reaction: Normal Operation
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: NA
Set DTC in HVDCDC: Yes
Vehicle reaction: Normal Operation
At Level 1:
Fault Severity: MEDIUM
Fault categorization guide: HVDCDC or the vehicle needs to derates its performance to mitigate the failure effects.
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X2 " Level 1"
Debounce strategy: HVDCDC shall apply appropriate debounce strategy (e.g. counter-based or timer-based) to confirm the fault. This is required to prevent fault toggling or serve system rection on false diagnostic, for robustness.
HVDCDC Fault Reaction: Derated Operation (if applicable).
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: Self-heatable. The HVDCDC shall unlatch the Level 1 fault rection as soon as the failure is not present( diagnostic tests pass, debounce may apply)
Set DTC in HVDCDC: Yes
Vehicle reaction: Derated operation. Display warning to driver.
At Level 2:
Fault Severity: HIGH
Fault categorization guide:
1) HVDCDC needs to be disabled to protect itself; and / or
2) The 400v DC Link needs to be discharged to protect the 400v HV components; and/ or
3) The vehicle needs to open HV battery contactor to isolate the failure effects from the rest of HV components.
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X3 " Level 2"
Debounce strategy: HVDCDC shall apply appropriate debounce strategy (e.g. counter-based or timer-based) to confirm the fault. This is required to prevent fault toggling or serve system rection on false diagnostic, for robustness.
HVDCDC Fault Reaction:
Depending on failure modes the HVDCDC power shall be disabled or expect the EDSU to command it to be disabled safety. The HVDCDC shall actively discharge the 400V DC link when possible.
Keep CAN communication.
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: Non Self-heatable. The HVDCDC shall latch the Level 2 fault rection until the unit sleeps or the unit receives the unlatch from the vehicle control.
Set DTC in HVDCDC: Yes
Vehicle reaction: Depending on the failure modes, the vehicle will command the HV systems to be disabled immediately or when its safe to do so. Vehicle limhome when possible. Display warning to driver.
safety_requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN Signal.
safety_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
safety_requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
safety_requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
safety_requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

safety_requirements.logical_block: Requirement to raise level 2 fault if HVDCDC detects all 16 channels of ADC failed to read input.
safety_requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise level 2 fault if ADC shall failed to get input or read input on all 16 channels.
safety_requirements.inputs:
1. All ADC channel not getting proper input
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
safety_requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU with respective Fault ID
safety_requirements.process:
The HVDCDC application shall set the state as NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown = 0x2 “Emergency_FaultyShutdown when the below conditions are met,
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. The power module status shall be Enabled.
3. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
4. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU with respective Fault ID
safety_requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU with respective Fault ID

safety_requirements.logical_block: Requirement to perform Power On Safe Test for checking the safety of the system.
safety_requirements.purpose: After IGN ON is detected, the HVDCDC shall perform Power On Safe Test for checking the safety of the system before start of operation
safety_requirements.inputs:
Provide the NM message over CAN.
OR
The IGN ON shall be provided to HVDCDC.
safety_requirements.outputs:
1. The HVDCDC shall perform Power On Safe Test which includes below test,
- Verifying that the HVDCDC shall receive proper voltage at each terminal of power board as well as controller board
- Verify the individual Communication modules like SPI, I2C, UART, CAN etc shall operational
- Verify that the Temperature Sensors and current circuitry shall function properly
- Verifying the integrity of stored data in non-volatile memory used by the ECU for storing calibration parameters and diagnostic trouble codes.
safety_requirements.process:
NM message’s shall be received over CAN.
The HVDCDC shall wakeup from Sleep state.
The HVDCDC shall start performing power on self test.
Verifying that the HVDCDC shall receive proper voltage at each terminal of power board as well as controller board.
Verify the individual Communication modules like SPI, I2C, UART, CAN etc shall operational.
Verify that the Temperature Sensors and current circuitry shall function properly.
Verifying the integrity of stored data in non-volatile memory used by the ECU for storing calibration parameters and diagnostic trouble codes.
safety_requirements.validation: Verify that the HVDCDC shall perform Power On Safe Test for checking the safety of the system before start of operation.

safety_requirements.logical_block: Requirement to support the Network DTC’s according to the MSL.01.04.0047 Diagnostic Network ID.
safety_requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall support the Network DTC’s according to the MSL.01.04.0047 Diagnostic Network ID Design Guide.
safety_requirements.inputs:
- Provide the CAN Communication Timeout fault by taking HVDCDC into bus-off conditions or excessive errors on the CAN bus so that acknowledge does not receive.
- Provide invalid data or missing message byte/bit which results in incorrect CRC. This will generate Invalid Safety Data check DTC.
- <Need to add for Invalid Safety check>
- Provide incorrect data length in message with respect to number of bytes transmitted over the message frame.
-The HVDCDC shall use the Network DTC’s as per MSL.01.04.0047 design guide.
safety_requirements.outputs:
-The HVDCDC shall raise below network DTC’s coming from DCU ZC1
1. CAN Communication Timeout (U180087)
2. Invalid Safety Data Check (U180183)
3. Invalid Safety Counter Check (U180182)
4. Invalid Data Length (U18018F)
safety_requirements.process:
HVDCDC shall list all the network DTC’s to be sent to primary controller.
The HVDCDC shall detect the network DTC’s mentioned in output
The HVDCDC shall report all network DTC’s mentioned in output
Store all the Network DTC’s into NVM
safety_requirements.validation:
Verify that the Network DTC’s shall be reported by HVDCDC to the Primary controller shall be as per the range P1E00xx to P1EFFxx.
Verify that the HVDCDC shall Send DTC over CAN.
Verify that the Status should return after successful store in NVM.
Verify that the DTC’s store in NVM after power on reset.

cybersecurity_requirements.logical_block: Requirement against detecting the Over temperature of Solid State Switch local sensor Temp measuring point by HVDCDC
cybersecurity_requirements.purpose: The Over temperature of Solid State Switch local sensor Temp measuring point shall be detected by the HVDCDC and raise a level 2 fault to avoid a cause of permanent damage to any of the surrounding HW components.
cybersecurity_requirements.inputs: Local VICOR sensor shall detect temperature above above HVDCDC_CAL specified temp range in degrees
cybersecurity_requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU should provide Fault ID when the Solid State Switch local sensor detects the temperature more than <TBD> degrees and this fault is raised after specified HVDCDC_CAL debounce time in msec
TVHDCDC5 @ HVDCDC_Status3 is greater than <TBD> degrees.
cybersecurity_requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall detect the over temperature of Solid State Switch local sensor Temp measuring point and raises a level 2 fault after specified HVDCDC_CAL debounce time in msec.
cybersecurity_requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU should provide Fault ID when the Solid State Switch local sensor detects the temperature more than <TBD> degrees

cybersecurity_requirements.logical_block: Response requirement against precharge takes longer than a calibratable time to complete.
cybersecurity_requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the precharge takes longer than a calibratable time to complete.
cybersecurity_requirements.inputs:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) shall On.
2. The 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module shall set to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V).
3. Ensure that NHVDCDCFault shall equal to 0x0 (no fault) on the CAN trace window.
4. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
5. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Enable" substate of the "HV_ON" state within 1.5 seconds, keep the low side EMI voltage (VHVDCDC400V_emi) less than half of the high side voltage(VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 10V(VPrecharge_C).
6. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Close_precharge_Ctt" substate of the "HV_ON" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
cybersecurity_requirements.outputs:
At Step 3:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x0 (“No Fault")
At Step 5:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 0x13(19) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_04_FAULT_IDt") and shall be raised after the fault response time of 2000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
cybersecurity_requirements.process: The application shall not transit from substate “Close_precharge_Ctt” to “Close_400V_SolidStateSw” state when low side voltage (VHVDCDC400V_emi) shall less than half of high side voltage(VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 10V(VPrecharge_C) and it transit to "Faulty" state.
cybersecurity_requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 0x13(19) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_04_FAULT_IDt")
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
cybersecurity_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
cybersecurity_requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
cybersecurity_requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
cybersecurity_requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

cybersecurity_requirements.logical_block: Response requirement against 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to open/close.
cybersecurity_requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to close or to open.
cybersecurity_requirements.inputs:
1. Provide short to high on Power board .
2. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with signal value as 0x00 ("Standby") via Canalyzer to HVDCDC application.
3. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable" substate of the "discharge" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V.
4. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Open_400V_SolidStateSw" substate of the "Discharge" state to the time when the level 2 fault shall be raised.
cybersecurity_requirements.outputs:
At Step 4:
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x5 ("Discharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw").
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 1("Close).
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x17(23) and is raised after the fault response time of 200ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
cybersecurity_requirements.process: The HVDCDC application shall transits to the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state from the “Open_400V_SolidStateSw” substate of the Discharge state when the solid state switch status confirms to be Closed.
cybersecurity_requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 0x17(23) and shall be raised after the fault response time of 200ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug shall set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")

metadata.source_section: Response requirement against 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to open/close.
metadata.evidence:
Term | Description
REQ ID | SWRS_Diag_59
Customer Req ID | SDS-HVDCDC-230
Milestone | B-Sample/PF2.0
Purpose1 | The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to close or to open.
Derived Requirement | No Such derived requirement is applicable.
Requirement Priority | Medium
Access Restrictions | No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
Input(s) | 1. Provide short to high on Power board .
2. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with signal value as 0x00 ("Standby") via Canalyzer to HVDCDC application.
3. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable" substate of the "discharge" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V.
4. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Open_400V_SolidStateSw" substate of the "Discharge" state to the time when the level 2 fault shall be raised.
Output(s) | At Step 4:
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x5 ("Discharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw").
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_s

cybersecurity_requirements.logical_block: Requirement to calculate and store the 4 bytes CVN (Calibration Verification Number).
cybersecurity_requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall calculate & Store the 4 Byte CVN (Calibration Verification Number) with in 120s of ECU power ON.
cybersecurity_requirements.inputs:
Calibration Verification Number (CVN) shall be of 4 byte.
CVN shall be read from D-Flash on ECU power ON.
CVN timeout parameter of 120 sec
cybersecurity_requirements.outputs:
Upon ECU startup, HVDCDC shall calculate the corresponding CVN value for the Calibration within 120sec of power on.
HVDCDC shall verify the calculated CVN with the CVN number stored in D-Flash.
- HVDCDC shall report CVN to primary controller.
cybersecurity_requirements.process:
Provide power on cycle to HVDCDC
HVDCDC shall calculate the 4 Byte CVN with in 120sec
HVDCDC shall read the CVN stored in NVM
HVDCDC shall validate the 4 Byte CVN with Stored value of CVN
HVDCDC shall update the respective CVN parameter
- HVDCDC shall report CVN to primary controller
cybersecurity_requirements.validation:
Verify that the time required for HVDCDC to calculate the corresponding CVN value for the Calibration is less than or equal to 120sec.
Verify that the 4-byte CVN shall be maintained by HVDCDC in the specified format.
Verify that the HVDCDC shall report verified CVN to primary controller continuously.
cybersecurity_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
cybersecurity_requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
cybersecurity_requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
cybersecurity_requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields are applicable.

interfaces.user: No specific GUI interface used for HVDCDC project.
interfaces.hardware:
1. CANCASEXL HW shall be connected to HVDCDC HW to check CAN communication.
2. UART Serial cable shall be connected to HVDCDC HW to observe Serial Consol logs.
3. CANCASEXL HW shall be connected to HVDCDC HW to flash application binary and calibration.hex.
4. MiniWiggler debugger shall be used to flash Primary and Secondary bootloader on HVDCDC
5. Power Supply with 12V shall be provided on IGN pin of HVDCDC controller board.
6. High Side Power Supply with 800V shall be connected to HVDCDC power board.
7. 8W Low Side Load shall be connected to HVDCDC power board.
8. DMM is used to observe 800V and 400V supply from power supply and Vicor IC respectively.
interfaces.software:
1. CANalyzer version 15 and 17 shall be used to check CAN communication with HVDCDC.
2. Teraterm or Similar tool will be used to observe Serial Consol logs.
3. PCOTApp tool shall be used to flash application binary and calibration.hex with bootloader implementation.
4. Infineon Memtool shall be used for flashing Primary and Secondary bootloader
5. Trace32 tool shall be used for debugging od SW code

acceptance_criteria:
1. McLaren and the EIC are jointly reviewing the software requirements & outcomes throughout the project.
2. Closure on review comments and feedback from McLaren within given schedule is the EIC’s responsibility.
3. SRS document shall be fulfilled all the SDS requirements provided by McLaren.
4. All the Functional, OBD, and all other non-functional requirements shall be clearly stated in SRS document.
5. All the faults and scenarios related requirements shall be properly written with detailed inputs and outputs expected.
6. Requirements comply with relevant ASPICE process requirements, industry standards, and regulatory requirements
7. The SRS document is under version control, and there is a process in place for managing changes and ensuring that all stakeholders have access to the latest version
operating_environment:
This section defines the operating conditions that describe the component status for each of the requirements defined herein.
Unless different operating conditions are specifically defined or referenced within a given requirement, requirements will reference specific Operating Conditions defined in Table 1, which define quantitative values for external factors that are anticipated to strongly influence the HV DCDC operation.
Operating Condition - Abbreviation | Operating Condition Description | DC Link High Voltage Input [V] | Coolant Inlet Temperature [degrees C] | Surrounding Component Temperature
[degrees C]
Installed | NOR | Normal Operation | 480-672 | -20 to +90 | -20 to +85
Installed | ND | Normal – Disabled | 480-672 | -40 to +90 | -40 to +85
Installed | LVO | Low Voltage Operation | 400-480 | -20 to +90 | -20 to +85
Not Installed | SHP | Shipping | 0 | N/A | -50 to +95
Not Installed | SRV | Service | 0 | N/A | -50 to +95
assumptions: 1. Any changes in the Hardware will affect the SW requirements
2. Any changes in requirements, where it deviates from the requirement finalized during the requirement phase, will be considered as a Change Request
3. Any changes in requirements related to the standards other than the mentioned in section 8, will be considered as a change request.
4. Some of the requirements await information from MAL(e.g. OBD), those are added with generic statements. Which will be later on updated after receiving the appropriate information.
open_questions: A separate QTS document is maintained at share-point for queries.

requirements.logical_block: Vehicle & Debug CAN communication on HVDCDC
requirements.purpose:
The HVHVDCDC shall integrate the low CAN drivers provided by a third party. The HVHVDCDC shall support below two CAN interfaces:
1. MCAN interface with LV connector (Vehicle communication)
2. DCAN interface with LV connector (Debug communication)
requirements.inputs:
For MCAN Interface : CAN stack/ARXML provided by a third party.
For DCAN Interface : Document having information of DCAN messages
requirements.outputs:
HVDCDC device has communication handler which handles the system status transmission over CAN.
MCAN interfaces for communication with vehicle EDSU. The HVHVDCDC broadcast the operating modes, fault levels, HVIL status, DC link currents and voltages on both the 800V side and 400V side, and temperatures etc. over MCAN.
DCAN interfaces for communication with vehicle diagnostic port. The HVHVDCDC broadcast the information reported by the power module, the internal state of the state machine transition, 400V side solid state switch status, precharge contactor status, 400V side active discharge switch status, internal fault ID, and Calibration parameters etc. over DCAN.
requirements.process:
1. Set 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module greater than 480V.
2. After voltage stabilize at 480V, wait for a duration of 200ms
3. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 (Enable) using MCAN interface
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN networks
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Integration of DBC or ARXML on HVDCDC
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall integrate communication extract (DBC or ARXML file) provided by customer.
requirements.inputs:
For MCAN Interface: CAN stack/ARXML provided by a third party.
For DCAN Interface: Document having information of DCAN messages
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed on the MCAN interface
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x1 ("Normal State")
Below CAN messages are observed on the DCAN interface
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is equal to 0x6 ("Normal state")
requirements.process:
1. Set 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module greater than 480V.
2. After voltage stabilize at 480V, wait for a duration of 200ms
3. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 (Enable) using MCAN interface

requirements.logical_block: Invalid CAN signal attributes or SNA requirements.
requirements.purpose:
Each CAN Signal received or transmitted by the HVDCDC shall have a definition of "Signal not available", which indicates that the signal content is invalid.
For physical quantities, "SNA" shall be defined as the highest positive number.
For state signals, "SNA" shall be defined as a dedicated state, with the name "Unknown" or "SNA" or "Invalid".
requirements.inputs: CAN signal parameter without “SNA” validation
requirements.outputs:
Status of signal for which value is set to its maximum value is set to SNA(Invalid_SingalName_NW).
Here, SignalName is the respective signal name from PDU structure
requirements.process: Software shall send the received fault/status information to MCAN.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: CAN signal level upper limit requirements on HVDCDC.
requirements.purpose: All transmit CAN signals shall be capped to a maximum value of "SNA" -1. This is so that "SNA" can be differentiated from signal railed at upper limit.
requirements.inputs: CAN signal parameter without “SNA” validation. The Maximum value should be "SNA" -1
requirements.outputs:
Status of signal for which value is set to its maximum value is set to SNA-1.
Here, SignalName is the respective signal name from PDU structure
requirements.process: Observe that the Software shall send the maximum value of SNA-1 over the MCAN.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
Verify that the maximum value of all the transmit CAN signals shall be SNA-1
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.logical_block: ‘Operation’ commands receive feature on HVDCDC
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall must be able to receive operation commands from the vehicle's CAN network.
requirements.inputs:
Vehicle CAN transmit 0x0 "Standby” operation command to the HVDCDC
Vehicle CAN transmit 0x1 "Enable" operation command to the HVDCDC
Vehicle CAN transmit 0x2 "Safe State" operation command to the HVDCDC
requirements.outputs: HVHVDCDC should execute these commands as per the command function.
requirements.process:
Vehicle CAN broadcast the 0x0 "Standby” operation command to the HVHVDCDC go into the Standby mode.
Vehicle CAN broadcast the 0x1 "Enable" operation command to the HVHVDCDC go into the Enable mode.
Vehicle CAN broadcast the 0x2 "Safe State" operation command to the HVHVDCDC go into the Safe State mode.
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN networks.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Fault unlatching request over CAN on HVDCDC.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC must be able to receive a fault unlatching request from the vehicle's CAN network.
requirements.inputs: Vehicle CAN transmit 0x1 "unlatch" request to the HVDCDC for fault unlatching.
requirements.outputs: In HVHVDCDC a fault is "latched", the act of "unlatching" removes the system ability to forcefully continue to broadcast the active fault state.
requirements.process: Vehicle CAN broadcast the 0x1 "unlatch" request to HVDCDC for the clear fault in Fault unlatching state.
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN networks.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Operating mode status over CAN on HVDCDC.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall broadcast the operating mode status to the vehicle's CAN network as per specified in SharePoint PDU cycle time.
requirements.inputs:
HVHVDCDC shall broadcast below operating mode status to the vehicle's CAN network
0x0 "Standby"
0x1 "Normal"
0x2 "Enable power module"
0x3 "Precharge 400V"
0x4 "Disable_power_module"
0x5 "Discharge 400V"
0x6 "Faulty"
requirements.outputs: In HVDCDC Software the all-state will reflect the operating mode status when they are working.
requirements.process:
The HVDCDC Software working at Standby Phase the status will reflect 0x0 "Standby" mode.
The HVDCDC Software working at HV_ON Enabling Phase the status will reflect 0x2 ”Enable Power Module” mode.
The HVDCDC Software working at HV_ON Precharge Phase the status will reflect 0x3 “ Precharge 400v” mode.
The HVDCDC Software working at HV_ON Normal Phase the status will reflect 0x1 ”Normal” mode.
The HVDCDC Software working at Discharge Disabling the converter power module Phase the status will reflect 0x4 "Disable_power_module" mode.
The HVDCDC Software working at Discharge Phase (Open the solid state switch on the 400V side, Actively discharge 400V side DC Link, Disable active discharge on 400V side DC Link) the status will reflect 0x5 ” Discharge 400V” mode.
HVDCDC Application at Fault_Unlatching and Faulty phase the status will reflects 0x6 “Faulty” mode.
requirements.validation:
CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN Signal.
UART to check operating mode status.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Fault levels over CAN on HVDCDC.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall broadcast the fault levels to the vehicle's CAN network.
requirements.inputs:
The HVHVDCDC shall broadcast the fault levels arises in state machine are given below:
NHVDCDCFault =
0X0 "No Fault"
0X1 "Level 0"
0X2 "Level 1"
0X3 "Level 2"
requirements.outputs: The HVHVDCDC shall detect the fault and the fault levels to the vehicle's CAN network.
requirements.process:
The HVHVDCDC shall detect the Level 2 fault in the state machine at following condition : HVDCDC needs to be disabled to protect itself / The 400v DC Link needs to be discharged to protect the 400v HV components / The vehicle needs to open HV battery contactor to isolate the failure effects from the rest of HV components. The HVHVDCDC send NHVDCDCFault = 0X3 "Level 2" message over vehicle's CAN.
The HVHVDCDC shall detect the Level 1 fault in the state machine at following condition : HVDCDC or the vehicle needs to derates its performance to mitigate the failure effects. The HVHVDCDC send NHVDCDCFault = 0X2 "Level 1" message over vehicle's CAN.
The HVHVDCDC shall detect the Level 0 fault in the state machine at following condition : The failure effects are invisible to rest of HV system / driver and HVDCDC or vehicle do not have to alternate its performance to mitigate the failure. The HVHVDCDC send NHVDCDCFault = 0X1 "Level 0" message over vehicle's CAN.
The HVHVDCDC shall detect the No Fault in the state machine. The HVHVDCDC send NHVDCDCFault = 0X1 "No Fault" message over vehicle's CAN.
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN Signal.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Measured DC link currents and voltages on 400V side over CAN on HVDCDC.
requirements.purpose:
The HVHVDCDC applies the below sign rules for DC link current measurements on both the 800V and 400V sides.
A positive value indicates buck operation (HV battery discharge).
A negative value indicates boost mode (HV battery charging).
requirements.inputs:
IHVDCDC800V
IHVDCDC400V
requirements.outputs:
The HVHVDCDC measured DC link current at HV battery discharging:
+(IHVDCDC800V)
+(IHVDCDC400V)
The HVHVDCDC measured DC link current at HV battery charging:
-(IHVDCDC800V)
-(IHVDCDC400V)
requirements.process:
The HVHVDCDC measured DC link current , When HV battery discharging & starts current flowing in ‘+’ direction towards VICOR module is +(IHVDCDC800V) and away from VICOR module is +(IHVDCDC400V)
The HVHVDCDC measured DC link current , When HV battery charging & starts current flowing in ‘-’ direction towards VICOR module is -(IHVDCDC400V) and away from VICOR module is -(IHVDCDC800V)
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN Signals.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: SWRS_COMM_08
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Broadcast the measured critical component temperatures over CAN on HVDCDC.
requirements.purpose:
The HVHVDCDC shall broadcast the measured critical component temperatures to the vehicle's CAN network using the below signals for the EDSU to provide derating protection.
THVDCDC1
THVDCDC2
THVDCDC3
THVDCDC4
THVDCDC5
requirements.inputs:
Temperatures of the critical components:
1) Temperature of the 800V EMI filter. (Temp_EMI_800V)
2) Temperature of the VICOR Module. (Temp_Vicor_A)
3) Temperature of the VICOR Module. (Temp_Vicor_B)
4) Temperature of the Solid-State Switch. (Temp_SSS)
5) Temperature of the 400V EMI filter. (Temp_EMI_400V)
requirements.outputs: Measured Temperatures of the critical components sent to the vehicle's CAN network.
requirements.process:
The temperature of the VICOR Module (Temp_Vicor_B) me) is measured directly by the ADC_SPI to the controller.
The temperature of the 800V EMI filter (Temp_EMI_800V) and the Temperature of the 400V EMI filter (Temp_EMI_400V) measured SPI-ADC to the controller.
Temperature of the Solid-State Switch. (Temp_SSS) measured SPI-ADC to the controller.
All measured temperatures are sent to the vehicle's CAN network.
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN Signal.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.logical_block: Vehicle CAN average cycle time of all PDUs over CAN
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall have vehicle CAN average cycle time of all PDUs in the network must be within ±2% of the specified PDU cycle time with a maximum of ±10 ms.
requirements.inputs: Required CAN cycle time for each PDU given by the customer
requirements.outputs:
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_Debug_PDU PDU is within 10ms(±2%).
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_NBM PDU is within 20ms(±2%).
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_Status1 PDU is within 10ms(±2%).
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_Status2 PDU is within 20ms(±2%).
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_Status3 PDU is within 1000ms.(±2%).
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_Status4 PDU is within 20ms.(±2%).
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDC_Status5 PDU is within 20ms.(±2%)
The average PDU cycle time for HVDCDCPin_Sts PDU is within 20ms.(±2%)
requirements.process:
Observe the delta t time within consecutive frames for all CAN signals using the CANalyzer tool by monitoring the minimum and maximum time for the below-mentioned PDUs,
HVDCDC_Debug_PDU
HVDCDC_Status1
HVDCDC_Status2
HVDCDC_Status3
HVDCDC_Status4
HVDCDC_Status5
HVDCDCPin_Sts

0x0 "Standby"

0x4 "PrechargeLS_CloseSolidStateSw"
0x5 "PrechargeLS_OpenPrechargeCtt"
0x6 "Normal"
0x7 "Shutdown_DisablePM"
0x8 "Shutdown_EnableAD400V"
0x9 "Shutdown_DisableAD400V"
0xA "Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw"
0xB "Faulty_OpenSolidStateSw"
0xC "Faulty_Enable400VAD"
0xD "Faulty_Disable400VAD"
0xE "Faulty_DisablePM"
0X0F "FaultyUnlatch_ClearPM"
0x10 "FaultyUnlatch_ClearSys"

requirements.logical_block: Fault levels to the vehicle CAN network for defining the faults and its reaction on CAN.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall have fault level categorization, diagnostic debounces, fault reactions on HVDCDC system and the vehicle, and the CAN interface for HVDCDC to report the fault level to the vehicle CAN network.
requirements.inputs: The fault levels to the vehicle CAN network for defining the faults and its reaction on HVDCDC system Or Vehicle.
requirements.outputs: The HVHVDCDC CAN Output NHVDCDCFault
requirements.process:
At No Fault:
Fault Severity: NA
Fault categorization guide: NA
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X0 "No Fault"
Debounce strategy: NA
HVDCDC Fault Reaction: Normal Operation
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: NA
Set DTC in HVDCDC: NA
Vehicle reaction: Normal Operation
At Level 0:
Fault Severity: LOW
Fault categorization guide:
1) The failure effects are invisible to rest of HV system / driver; and
2) HVDCDC or vehicle do not have to alternate its performance to mitigate the failure.
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X1 " Level 0"
Debounce strategy: HVDCDC shall apply appropriate debounce strategy (e.g. counter-based or timer-based) to confirm the fault. This is required to prevent fault toggling or serve system rection on false diagnostic, for robustness.
HVDCDC Fault Reaction: Normal Operation
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: NA
Set DTC in HVDCDC: Yes
Vehicle reaction: Normal Operation
At Level 1:
Fault Severity: MEDIUM
Fault categorization guide: HVDCDC or the vehicle needs to derates its performance to mitigate the failure effects.
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X2 " Level 1"
Debounce strategy: HVDCDC shall apply appropriate debounce strategy (e.g. counter-based or timer-based) to confirm the fault. This is required to prevent fault toggling or serve system rection on false diagnostic, for robustness.
HVDCDC Fault Reaction: Derated Operation (if applicable).
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: Self-heatable. The HVDCDC shall unlatch the Level 1 fault rection as soon as the failure is not present( diagnostic tests pass, debounce may apply)
Set DTC in HVDCDC: Yes
Vehicle reaction: Derated operation. Display warning to driver.
At Level 2:
Fault Severity: HIGH
Fault categorization guide:
1) HVDCDC needs to be disabled to protect itself; and / or
2) The 400v DC Link needs to be discharged to protect the 400v HV components; and/ or
3) The vehicle needs to open HV battery contactor to isolate the failure effects from the rest of HV components.
HVDCDC CAN Output: NHVDCDCFault = 0X3 " Level 2"
Debounce strategy: HVDCDC shall apply appropriate debounce strategy (e.g. counter-based or timer-based) to confirm the fault. This is required to prevent fault toggling or serve system rection on false diagnostic, for robustness.
HVDCDC Fault Reaction:
Depending on failure modes the HVDCDC power shall be disabled or expect the EDSU to command it to be disabled safety. The HVDCDC shall actively discharge the 400V DC link when possible.
Keep CAN communication.
HVDCDC Fault Recovery Mechanism: Non Self-heatable. The HVDCDC shall latch the Level 2 fault rection until the unit sleeps or the unit receives the unlatch from the vehicle control.
Set DTC in HVDCDC: Yes
Vehicle reaction: Depending on the failure modes, the vehicle will command the HV systems to be disabled immediately or when its safe to do so. Vehicle limhome when possible. Display warning to driver.
requirements.validation: CANape or CAN BUS Analyzer is a software tool Validating CAN Signal.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Broadcast critical power module PMBus output information on CAN.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall broadcast the critical debug CAN information of SDS Requirements (SDS-HVDCDC-104, 105, 106,107,108) onto the vehicle CAN network for development purpose.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set KL30 and KL15(IGN) to On
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
3. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface
4. Once the HVDCDC application is reached in "Normal" mode send NHVDCDCCommand as to 0x00 ("Standby") using the CAN interface
5. Once the HVDCDC application is reached in "Standby" mode send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface
6. Once the HVDCDC application reached into "Normal" mode send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x02 ("Safe State") using the CAN interface
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
1) NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x0 (Standby)
2)CAN messages are observed sequentially on the MCAN interface
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x2 ("Enable power module")
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Precharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x1 ("Normal")
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x4("Disable_power_module")
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x5 (“Discharge 400V”)
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x6 (“Faulty")
requirements.process: The HVHVDCDC broadcasts the operating mode status to the vehicle's CAN network as per specified in SharePoint PDU cycle time.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool

requirements.logical_block: Broadcast all the information reported by the power module onto the debug CAN.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall broadcast all the information reported by the power module onto the debug CAN.
requirements.inputs:
1. Check for signals HVDCDC_VED_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts and HVDCDC_VICOR_FLT @ HVDCDC_NBM2_PDU on DCAN interface.
2. Check for CAN signals iHSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, vHSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, iLSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, vLSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, THVDCDCNBM @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU and NHVDCDCNBMSts @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU on DCAN interface.
3. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
4. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
requirements.outputs:
1) Received value for signals HVDCDC_VED_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts is equal to 0 on Vehicle CAN and HVDCDC_VICOR_FLT @ HVDCDC_NBM2_PDU is equal to 0 on DCAN.
2) Received value for signals iHSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, vHSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, iLSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, vLSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, THVDCDCNBM @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU and NHVDCDCNBMSts @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU on DCAN interface all equal to 0.
3) Received high side current value on signal iHSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, received high side voltage on signal vHSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, received low side current on signal iLSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, received low side voltage on signal vLSHVDCDC @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU, received VICOR temperature value on signal THVDCDCNBM @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU and receive value equal to 0 on signal NHVDCDCNBMSts @ HVDCDC_PM_I2C_Info_PDU on DCAN interface.

requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall broadcast all the ADC channels readings onto the debug CAN.

requirements.logical_block: Reporting status of control IO’s.
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall report the status of control io’s belows signals:
Discharge_ENc
Precharge_Enc
SSS_Enc
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface 200ms right after the 800V voltage stabilise at 480V
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
1) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface when the application is in the "PrechargeLS_ClosePrechargeCtt" substate of the "precharge 400V" state:
Over the DCAN interface,
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x3 ("PrechargeLS_ClosePrechargeCtt")
HVDCDC_SSS_ENc @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 0 ("OFF")
Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface when the application is in the "PrechargeLS_CloseSolidStateSw" substate of the "precharge 400V" state:
Over the DCAN interface,
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x4 ("PrechargeLS_CloseSolidStateSw")
HVDCDC_SSS_ENc @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 1 ("ON")
3) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface when the application is in the "Shutdown_OpenEFuse" substate of the "Discharge 400V" state:
Over the DCAN interface,
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is equal to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenEFuse")
HVDCDC_SSS_ENc @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 0 ("OFF")
requirements.process: The HVDCDC broadcasts the precharge contactor status using HVDCDC_Precharge_ENc onto the debug CAN.

requirements.logical_block: Application State: Sleep Requirement
requirements.purpose: The function Sleep/Wake shall put the HVDCDC into a sleep state, where it saves energy. It shall also provide a mechanism for waking the HVDCDC back up. Moreover, CAN network management is part of this function.
requirements.inputs: KL15 line shall be removed/OFF.
requirements.outputs:
1. HVDCDC shall go into sleep state.
2. All the peripherals shall achieve the state which is configured for sleep mode
requirements.process:
Detect KL15 line signal interrupt.
Verify the signal whether it is sleep signal.
Run the shutdown sequence.
Put PMIC IC into the standby mode.
The TriCore in shutdown state and only SCR core will be active.
requirements.validation:
Verify that the current consumption is reduced and lowest rate.
Verify that system shall entered sleep state.
Verify that all the peripherals shall be reached to the configured state/disable state
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Application State: Wakeup Requirement
requirements.purpose: Application shall be able to monitor the KL15 line to wake up the system
requirements.inputs: KL15 line must be up/ON
requirements.outputs:
1. HVDCDC should go to stand-by state after wakes up.
2. All the required peripherals shall be initialized after wakeup state is detected
requirements.process:
Detect KL15 line signal interrupt.
Verify the signal whether it is wake up signal.
SCR core shall wake up the TriCore.
TriCore shall be powered on and shall move to standby state.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
The HVDCDC Software shall set CAN Signal NHVDCDCStatus = 0x0 Standby in "Standby" state.
The HVDCDC Software shall set CAN Signal NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x0 Standby.

requirements.logical_block: Application State: Power module and HVDCDC Communication
requirements.purpose: Communication between the power module and HVDCDC via I2C shall be establish when the 800V DC link voltage is above a certain voltage level (HVDCDC_CAL)
requirements.inputs:
1. 800V_Vicor (high side) voltage of the power module shall be greater than 30V which can also be a calibratable value.
2. 800V_emi voltage of the power module shall be greater than 30V which can also be a calibratable value.
3. 800V_IND voltage of the power module shall be greater than 30V which can also be a calibratable value.
requirements.outputs:
1. Enabling HVDCDC power module.
NHVDCDCStatus = 0x2 Enable power module.
2. The power module shall establish communication with HVDCDC in all state except power down safe State.
3. Communication should establish with power module
requirements.process:
Below are the conditions establish communication between power module and HVDCDC
1. 800V side DC link voltage > 30V (HVDCDC_CAL) and
VHDCDC800V_Vicor > 30V (HVDCDC_CAL) and
VHDCDC800V_ind > 30V (HVDCDC_CAL)
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
The HVDCDC shall set CAN Signal as NHVDCDCStatus = 0x2 Enable power module.
The power module shall establish communication with HVDCDC in all state except power down self state.

requirements.logical_block: Application State: Monitor the status of VICOR
requirements.purpose: Application shall send query to VICOR module with defined interval using PMBus specification and monitor the status of VICOR
requirements.inputs: Application shall send the i2c query message to VICOR module to check the communication is established
requirements.outputs: VICOR module shall provide the response once optimum voltage level condition meets(800v) HV battery contactors are closed.

Below are the conditions for Enable state to get trigger,

2. NHVDCDCCommand = 0x01 ("Enable") Received
3. Power Module Initialized and Communication established

requirements.logical_block: HV_Initializtion state transition requirement.
requirements.purpose: The application shall be able to transit to the "HV_Initializtion state"
requirements.inputs:
1. Set 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module greater than 480V
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") from EDSU to HVDCDC using the CAN interface
requirements.outputs:
Application should be able to transition to the "HV_Initializtion state"
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatus = 0x8 "HV_Initializtion" state (On Vehicle CAN).
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0xF "HV_Initializtion" state (On Debug and Vehicle CAN).
requirements.process:
Below are the conditions for Enable state to get trigger?
1. 800V side DC link voltage > 480V (HVDCDC_CAL)
2. NHVDCDCCommand = 0x01 ("Enable") Received
3. Power Module Initialized and Communication established
4. NHVDCDCFault != 0x3 ("Level 2" fault)
requirements.validation:
1. The DCDC Power Module shall be enabled
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
2. The HVDCDC shall set CAN Signal as NHVDCDCStatus = 0x8 "HV_Initializtion" state.
3. The HVDCDC shall set CAN Signal as NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0xF "HV_Initializtion" state.

requirements.logical_block: Transition requirement for substate “Precharge” & and entering to “Close_precharge_Ctt” state.
requirements.purpose: The application shall be able to transit to substate “Precharge” and enters to “Close_precharge_Ctt” state
requirements.inputs: Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
requirements.outputs: Application state transitioned to “Close_precharge_Ctt” substate after 500msec
requirements.process:
Below are the conditions for Precharge state to get trigger:
The power module status confirms to be enabled.
The power module reports the 400V side output greater than a calibration threshold.
The EDSU command NHVDCDCCommand == 0x1 "Enable" remains.
- No Level 2 fault is present.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
The HVHVDCDC shall transit to "Precharge" state and set CAN Signal as NHVDCDCStatus = 0x3 Precharge 400V.
The HVHVDCDC shall close the 400V side precharge contactor.
The HVHVDCDC shall set CAN Signal as NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x3 PrechargeLS_ClosePrechargeCtt.

requirements.logical_block: Timeout in transition between Enable and Precharge results in Faulty state (Level 2 fault) of HVDCDC
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to the Faulty state (Level 2 fault) when timeout is detected while transition between Enable and Precharge.
requirements.inputs:
Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x11(17) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_02_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 2500ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
requirements.process:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) is On.
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
3. Ensure that NHVDCDCFault is equal to 0x0 (no fault) on the CAN trace window.
4. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
1. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
2. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x11(17) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_02_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 2500ms.
3. NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Transition requirement to Faulty_PowerDn-SafeState state when timeout between Open_PreCharge_Ctt and Select_Faulty_Shutdown_Sequence.
requirements.purpose: The application shall be able to transit to substate “Faulty_PowerDn-SafeState” upon Timeout between Open_PreCharge_Ctt and Select_Faulty_Shutdown_Sequence transition.
requirements.inputs:
Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
The power module status shall be Enabled.
Precharge contractor confirmed to be open
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to <TBD> ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_TIMEOUT1_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process:
The HVDCDC application does transits to the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state from the “Open_PreCharge_Ctt” substate of the "Faulty" state after the defined the calibratable timeout when the below conditions are met,
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. The power module status shall be Enabled.
3. Precharge contractor confirmed to be open 5. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
4. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to <TBD> ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_TIMEOUT1_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to <TBD> ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_TIMEOUT1_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Transition requirement to “Shutdown_Sequence_Completed” when Active Discharge switch status is confirmed as open.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to “Shutdown_Sequence_Completed” when Active Discharge switch status is confirmed as open.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
3. The power module status shall be disabled.
4. Disable active discharge on 400V side DC Link
5. Active Discharge switch status shall be confirmed as open
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed
1. NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x15 ("Faulty_Shutdown_Completed ")
2. The HVDCDC shall go into Sleep State
3. All the peripherals shall achieve the state which is configured for sleep mode
requirements.process:
The HVDCDC application does transits to the "Shutdown_Sequence_Completed" state from the “Disable_400V_AD” substate of the "Faulty" state after the defined the calibratable timeout when the below conditions are met,
1. The power module status shall be disabled.
2. Disable active discharge on 400V side DC Link
3. Active Discharge switch status shall be confirmed as open
4. NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x15 ("Faulty_Shutdown_Completed ")
5. The HVDCDC shall go into Sleep State

requirements.logical_block: Transition requirement to “Sleep State” when delay (100msec) elapsed and NM messaged is stopped receiving.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to “Sleep State” when delay (100msec) elapsed and NM messaged is stopped receiving.
requirements.inputs: 1. The HVDCDC shall stop receiving CAN NM message “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU”
requirements.outputs:
1. HVDCDC shall go into sleep state after specified delay timeout.
2. Within the specified delay, all the peripherals shall achieve the state configured for sleep mode. i.e shutdown sequence shall be reported.
3. The HVDCDC shall enable the CAN module for reception of only NM messages in Sleep mode. Application Message Transmission and Reception shall be stop on CAN bus.
requirements.process:
1. HVDCDC shall stop receiving CAN NM message
2. Within the specified delay, the shutdown sequence shall be reported.
3. HVDCDC should transition from any state (except Sleep state) to "Sleep" state of Application state machine.
4. Disable the peripherals before going into sleep mode.
5. The TriCore shall be in shutdown state and only SCR core will be active.
6. SCR core shall put the CAN transceiver into the standby mode to save more power
7. SCR core shall set CAN transceiver configuration to read only mode
requirements.validation:
Verify that HVDCDC shall enter into sleep state which can be monitored on serial console.
Verify that the current consumption is reduced on DSO or DMM.

requirements.logical_block: Pre-charge contactor control requirement.
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall command to open the pre-charge contactor if the check in reports The pre-charge contactor is closed.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x13 "Faulty_OpenPreChargeCtt".
requirements.inputs:
1. On the power board connect Pull-Up from TP411 to 5V
2. Send CAN signal NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd message as 0x02 ("Safe State") via CAN interface to HVDCDC application.
3. Check fault response time from the time when the system transits to "Open_PreCharge_Ctt" to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x13 ("Faulty_OpenPreChargeCt")
HVDCDC_PED_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 1 ("Enable").
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x1A(26) after the fault response time of 200ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application shall transits to substate "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" from substate “Open_PreCharge_Ctt” of the “Faulty” state when the precharge contractor is confirmed to be closed.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x13 "Faulty_OpenPreChargeCtt".

metadata.evidence:
Term | Description
REQ ID | SWRS_APP_STATE_22
Customer Req ID | SDS-HVDCDC-318
Milestone | B-Sample/PF2.0
Purpose1 | The HVDCDC shall command to open the pre-charge contactor if the check in reports The pre-charge contactor is closed.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x13 "Faulty_OpenPreChargeCtt".
Derived Requirement | No Such derived requirement is applicable.
Access Restrictions | No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
Input(s) | 1. On the power board connect Pull-Up from TP411 to 5V
2. Send CAN signal NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd message as 0x02 ("Safe State") via CAN interface to HVDCDC application.
3. Check fault response time from the time when the system transits to "Open_PreCharge_Ctt" to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
Output(s) | Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x13 ("Faulty_OpenPreChargeCt")
HVDCDC_PED_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 1 ("Enable").
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x1A(26) after the fault response time of 200ms.

requirements.logical_block: Transition requirement to "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state upon a HVDCDC level 2 fault and Faulty state not able to shutdown the system.
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall transit to "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state upon a HVDCDC level 2 fault and Faulty state not able to shutdown the system.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatus = 0x7 "PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW".
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x11 "PowerDown_Safe_State".
requirements.inputs: The HVDCDC shall transit to "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x6 (“Faulty State”)
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is equal to 0xE ("Faulty_DisablePM")
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x21(33) after the fault response time of 10000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process:
The HVDCDC application does transits to the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state from the “Disable_3" substate of the "Faulty" state after the defined timeout of 2ms when the below conditions are met,
1. VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 15V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than 175V(HVDCDC_CAL).
3. The power module status is Enabled.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatus = 0x7.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x11 "PowerDown_Safe_State".

requirements.logical_block: Exit requirement from "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state upon a sleep request.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall exit the "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state upon a sleep request.
requirements.inputs:
1) Keep the power module Enabled by shorting J101 pin 5 to high on the power board.
2) provide Ignition off by making KL15 off
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application shall not transit to sleep state from the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state when KL15 is turned off and power board turned on.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool

requirements.logical_block: Requirements for level 0 fault response.
requirements.purpose: Once a level 0 fault is triggered, the HVDCDC shall latch NHVDCDCFault = 0x1 "Level 0" for 5 seconds (HVDCDC_CAL) or until the fault trigger is no longer present, whichever is longer.
requirements.inputs: Send the invalid "VehDynState_PDU" signal for less than 5 seconds and again start sending the valid "VehDynState_PDU" signal.
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x1 ("Level 0")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x4B(75)
Below CAN messages are observed after 5 seconds,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x0 ("No fault")
requirements.process: Send the invalid "VehDynState_PDU" signal for less than 5 seconds and again start sending the valid "VehDynState_PDU" signal.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x1 ("Level 0")
Below CAN messages are observed after 5 seconds,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x0 ("No fault")

requirements.inputs:
1.VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 15V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than 175V(HVDCDC_CAL).
3. The power module status shall be Disabled.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x16(22) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_DISCHARGE_TIMEOUT_01_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")

3. The power module status shall be Disabled.

NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x16(22) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_DISCHARGE_TIMEOUT_01_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.

NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")

1. VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 15V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than 175V(HVDCDC_CAL).
3. The power module status shall be Disabled.
4. Open the solid state switch on 400V side
4. Disable active discharge on 400V side DC link
5. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
6. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x18(24) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_DISCHARGE_TIMEOUT_03_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x18(24) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_FAULTY_DISCHARGE_TIMEOUT_03_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.logical_block: Raise Level 0 fault when Timeout between Active_Discharge_400V and Disable_400V_AD.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise Level 0 fault when Timeout between Active_Discharge_400V and Disable_400V_AD.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with signal value as 0x02 ("Safe State") via Canalyzer to HVDCDC application.
2. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable_3" substate of the "Faulty" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V and give more than 60V to 400V bus(400V EMI)
3. Check the fault response time by checking the HVDCDC applications transition time from "Active_Discharge_400V" to "Disable_400V_AD" substate of the "Discharge" state.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed sequentially,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 70 and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
requirements.process:
1. VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 15V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than 175V(HVDCDC_CAL).
3. The power module status shall be Disabled.
4. Open the solid state switch on 400V side
5. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with signal value as 0x02 ("Safe State") via Canalyzer to HVDCDC application.
6. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable_3" substate of the "Faulty" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V and give more than 60V to 400V bus(400V EMI)
7. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
8. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 70 and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 70 and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.

requirements.logical_block: Transition requirement to the Open_400V_SolidStateSw_ 2 state when Emergency_FaultyShutdown is provided.
requirements.purpose: The application shall be able to transit to substate “Open_400V_SolidStateSw_ 2” state When Emergency_FaultyShutdown is provided.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
3. The power module status shall be Enabled.
4. The precharge contactor is confirmed to be open
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x12 ("Faulty_Enable400VAD_NHVDCDCStatusDebug_NW ")
NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x2 ("Emergency_FaultyShutdown")

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to transit into "Fault Unlatching" state if all the conditions are met.
requirements.purpose:
When the value of the signal NFaultUnlatchRequestHVDCDC transition to "Clear Fault Requested (0x1)" when the HVDCDC is brought to or latched in a non-hazardous status due to a level 2 fault, the HVDCDC shall evaluate if the fault unlatching is permitted by evaluating if the following conditions are met:
- 400V DC link voltage < HVDCDC_CAL V
- the power module is disabled
- the precharge contactor status is open
- the 400V solid state switch status is open

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for transition to sleep
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transition to sleep, if the HVDCDC stops receiving the NM messages.
requirements.inputs: 1. The HVDCDC shall stop receiving CAN NM message “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU”
requirements.outputs:
1. HVDCDC shall go into sleep state after CAN NM message timeout.
2. All the peripherals shall achieve the state which is configured for sleep mode
3. The HVDCDC shall enable the CAN module for reception of only NM messages in Sleep mode. Application Message Transmission and Reception shall be stop on CAN bus
requirements.process:
1. HVDCDC shall stop receiving CAN NM message
2. CAN NM message timeout shall get overflow
3. HVDCDC should transition from "Repeat Message" state to the "Ready Sleep State" of Network Management.
4. HVDCDC should transition from any state (except Sleep state) to "Sleep" state of Application state machine.
5. Disable the peripherals before going into sleep mode.
6. The TriCore shall be in shutdown state and only SCR core will be active.
7. SCR core shall put the CAN transceiver into the standby mode to save more power
8. SCR core shall set CAN transceiver configuration to read only mode
requirements.validation:
Verify that HVDCDC shall enter into sleep state which can be monitor on serial console.
Verify that the current consumption is reduced on DSO or DMM.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Response requirements to no NM messages on vehicle CAN.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transition to sleep state when no NM messages on the vehicle CAN network.
requirements.inputs:
1. The HVDCDC shall stops receiving CAN NM message “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU”
2. The timer NM_MSG_RECEIVE shall be elapsed
requirements.outputs:
1. HVDCDC shall go into sleep state after NM_MSG_RECEIVE timeout.
2. All the peripherals shall achieve the state which is configured for sleep mode
3. The HVDCDC shall enable the CAN module for reception of only NM messages in Sleep mode. Application Message Transmission and Reception shall be stop on CAN bus
requirements.process:
1. HVDCDC shall stop receiving CAN NM message
2. The timer NM_MSG_RECEIVE shall get overflow
3. HVDCDC should transition from "Repeat Message" state to the "Ready Sleep State" of Network Management.
4. HVDCDC should transition from "Ready Sleep" state to the "Prepare Bus Sleep Mode" of Network Management upon CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME overflow.
5. HVDCDC should transition from "Prepare Bus Sleep” Mode to the "Bus Sleep Mode" of Network Management upon “CANNM_WAIT_BUS_SLEEP_TIME” overflow. (Need to get details for PDU for NM States from Sanket)
6. Disable the peripherals before going into sleep mode.
7. HVDCDC should transition from any state (except Sleep state) to "Sleep" state of Application state machine.
8. The TriCore shall be in shutdown state and only SCR core will be active.
9. SCR core shall put the CAN transceiver into the standby mode to save more power
10. SCR core shall set CAN transceiver configuration to read only mode
requirements.validation:
Verify that HVDCDC shall transition to the "Bus Sleep” Mode. (Check with Sanket if any PDU/Signal available for this)
Verify that the current consumption is reduced on DSO or DMM.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

1. HVDCDC shall stop receiving CAN NM message
2. The timer NM_MSG_RECEIVE shall get overflow
3. HVDCDC should transition from "Repeat Message" state to the "Ready Sleep State" of Network Management upon NM_REPEAT_MESSAGE_TIME overflow.
4. HVDCDC should transition from "Ready Sleep" state to the "Prepare Bus Sleep Mode" of Network Management upon CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME overflow.
5. The HVDCDC shall write all the required data one by one into NVM
6. HVDCDC shall return status as NVM_WRITE_SUCCESSFUL for each write operation
7. Disable the peripherals before going into sleep mode.
8. HVDCDC should transition from any state (except Sleep state) to "Sleep" state of Application state machine.
9. The TriCore shall be in shutdown state and only SCR core will be active.
10. SCR core shall put the CAN transceiver into the standby mode to save more power
11. SCR core shall set CAN transceiver configuration to read only mode
requirements.validation: Verify the HVDCDC shall successfully complete NvM write operation for all the relevant data.
