requirements.logical_block: Requirements for HVDCDC NM state machine.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall follow below mention NM state machine.
requirements.inputs:
The HVDCDC shall support below NM State transition:
1. The HVDCDC shall transition from Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state
2. The HVDCDC shall transition from Repeat Message state to Ready Sleep State
3. The HVDCDC shall transition from Ready Sleep State to Prepare Bus Sleep Mode
4. The HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Bus Sleep Mode
5. The HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state
The HVDCDC shall set below configuration parameters:
1. NM_MSG_RECEIVE_TIME shall be configurable with value HVDCDC_CAL.
2. NM_REPEAT_MESSAGE_TIME shall be configurable with value HVDCDC_CAL.
3. CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME shall be configurable with value HVDCDC_CAL.
4. CANNM_WAIT_BUS_SLEEP_TIME shall be configurable with value HVDCDC_CAL.
5. NM_MSG_PR_RECEIVE_TIME shall be configurable with value HVDCDC_CAL.
requirements.outputs:
1. The HVDCDC shall transition from Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state after Wake-up message reception
2. The HVDCDC shall transition from Repeat Message state to Ready Sleep State after CANNM_REPEAT_MESSAGE_TIME elapsed
3. The HVDCDC shall transition from Ready Sleep State to Prepare Bus Sleep Mode after CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME elapsed
4. The HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Bus Sleep Mode after CANNM_WAIT_BUS_SLEEP_TIME elapsed
5. The HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state after Wake-up message reception
requirements.process:
1. The HVDCDC shall transition from Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state when NM message received with in NM_MSG_RECEIVE_TIME
2. The HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state when NM message received with in NM_MSG_RECEIVE_TIME
3. The HVDCDC shall transition from Repeat Message state to Ready Sleep State upon NM_REPEAT_MESSAGE_TIME overflow
4. The HVDCDC shall transition from Ready Sleep State to Prepare Bus Sleep Mode upon CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME overflow
5. The HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Bus Sleep Mode upon CANNM_WAIT_BUS_SLEEP_TIME overflow
requirements.validation:
1. Verify that the HVDCDC shall transition from Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state when NM message received with in NM_MSG_RECEIVE_TIME
2. Verify that the HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state when NM message received with in NM_MSG_RECEIVE_TIME
3. Verify that the HVDCDC shall transition from Repeat Message state to Ready Sleep State upon NM_REPEAT_MESSAGE_TIME overflow
4. Verify that the HVDCDC shall transition from Ready Sleep State to Prepare Bus Sleep Mode upon CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME overflow
5. Verify that the HVDCDC shall transition from Prepare Bus Sleep Mode to Bus Sleep Mode upon CANNM_WAIT_BUS_SLEEP_TIME overflow
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirements to configure HVDCDC as NM passive mode.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall configure as NM passive mode.
requirements.inputs: 1.The HVDCDC shall only receive “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU” message.
requirements.outputs:
1.The HVDCDC shall only receive “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU” message in low power mode.
2.The HVDCDC shall not transmit any CAN NM and Application message
3.The HVDCDC shall transit from Bus Sleep Mode to Repeat Message state
requirements.process:
1. The HVDCDC shall be in SCR core on Power on reset
2. The HVDCDC shall be configured as CAN receiver Mode.
3. The HVDCDC shall be continuously monitor the NM wakeup signal “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU”
requirements.validation:
1.Verify that HVDCDC should be receive only NM CAN messages “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU”.
2.Verify that HVDCDC should not transmit any NM CAN and Application messages.

requirements.logical_block: Requirements to start the transmit and receive CAN messages after the Bus communication.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall start the transmit and receive CAN messages after the Bus communication” NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU” Received
requirements.inputs: 1. The HVDCDC shall receive CAN NM message (Bus Communication = Received) “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU” with wakeup value(TBD).

2. HVDCDC should transition from "Sleep" state to the "Standby" state of Application.
3.HVDCDC shall receive Nm CAN message
4. Application shall start transmitting and receive application CAN messages
requirements.process:
1. Start the NM_MSG_RECEIVE_TIME.
2. HVDCDC should update the state NM State = Repeat Message state of Network Management.
3. HVDCDC should update the state App State = Standby state of Application State Machine.
4.Application Should start the CAN Tx/Rx Messages.
requirements.validation:
1. Verify that HVDCDC shall transition from "Bus Sleep” Mode to the "Repeat Message" state
2. Verify that HVDCDC shall Transmit and receive the CAN messages.
3. Verify that HVDCDC shall transition from "Sleep" state to the "Standby" state of Application.

requirements.logical_block: Requirements to suppress all application messages.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall suppress all application messages after the state transition between “Ready Sleep" state to the "Prepare Bus Sleep” Mode
requirements.inputs: 1. The HVDCDC shall transit from "Ready Sleep" state to the "Prepare Bus Sleep” Mode
requirements.outputs: 1. HVDCDC shall be stop the application CAN Tx/Rx messages after the transit from Ready sleep state to Prepare Bus Sleep Mode
requirements.process:
1. Start the parameter CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME.
2. Increment the CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME counter
3. Verify that CANNM_TIMEOUT_TIME is overflow
4. Set the NM state = Prepare Bus Sleep Mode
5. Disable the application CAN Tx/Rx messages
6. HVDCDC shall only receive the CAN NM “NMZC1Message_Hybrid_ASR_PDU” PDU
requirements.validation:
1. Verify that HVDCDC shall transition from " Ready sleep state " to the " Prepare Bus Sleep” Mode
2. Verify that HVDCDC shall not Transmit and Receive the application CAN messages .

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against Level 2 fault if the vehicle CAN message EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd has timed out for a calibratable time.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a Level 2 fault if the vehicle CAN message EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd has timed out for a calibratable time.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms
2. Diagnostics shall be done in all state except Sleep state and Power down safe state.
3. System will set the level 2 fault for the CAN communication timeout
4. The Timeout parameter shall be calibratable.
5. Diagnostics shall be done in every 10msec.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
A. Initial status when no fault present
- NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x0 ("No Fault")
- NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x0 ("No fault")
B. Status when fault detected
- NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
- NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 36(0x24) ("Time out NHVDCDCCommand") and is raised after the fault response time of 500ms.
C. The level 2 fault should be raised in All States except Sleep state and Power down Safe state.
D. DTC for this fault shall be provided as per DTC table and store it in NVM before going into sleep mode.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against vehicle CAN in Bus Off State
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a Level 2 fault if the vehicle CAN is in Bus Off State.
requirements.inputs:
1. Remove Vehicle CAN connection on the CAN BUS.
2. Diagnostics shall be done in all state except Sleep state and Power down safe state.
3. Diagnostics shall be done within the HVDCDC_CAL time.
requirements.outputs:
A. Initial status when no fault present
- NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x0 ("No Fault")
- NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x0 ("No fault")
B. Status when fault detected
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2 fault")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 81 and this fault shall raise after 50ms.
C. The level 2 fault should be raised in All States except Sleep state and Power down Safe state
requirements.process:
1. Run the Diagnostics for every 10msec.
2. Increment the CAN timeout counter if CAN communication does not present on vehicle CAN
3. Check for the CAN timeout counter overflow condition is match.
4. If the condition is match raise the level 2 fault with Fault ID 81.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2 fault")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 81 and this fault shall raise after 50ms.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against CAN message VehDynState time-out for a calibratable time
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall raise a Level 0 fault if the vehicle CAN messages VehDynState has timed out for a calibratable time.
The HVDCDC shall latch the last valid values received on these messages/signals until the communication resumes or a power cycle.
requirements.inputs:
1. Stop sending CAN Command VehDynState via CAN interface to HVDCDC applicationVehDynState.
2. Diagnostics shall be done in all state except Sleep state and Power down safe state.
3. System will set the level 2 fault for the CAN communication timeout
4. The Timeout parameter shall be calibratable.
5. Diagnostics shall be done every 10msec.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
A. Initial status when no fault present
- NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x0 ("No Fault")
- NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x0 ("No fault")
B. Status when fault detected
- NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
- NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 74 after fault response time of 1000ms.
C. The level 2 fault should be raised in All States except Sleep state and Power down Safe state
requirements.process:
1. Run the Diagnostics for every 10msec.
2. Increment the CAN timeout counter if VehDynState command in not received
3. Check for the CAN timeout counter overflow condition is match.
4. If the condition is match raise the level 2 fault with Fault ID 74.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
1. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 = 0
2. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU = 74

requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against 400V DC link current circuit failure & response
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the 400V DC link circuit current short to high failure raise a level 2 fault.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms.
2. The High side voltage should be greater than HVDCDC_CAL_DAIG_6 (480V).
3. Make necessary arrangement to provide Short to High failure at 400V side current shunt (low side).
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 Shall set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU Shall equal to 51 ("DIAGNOSTIC_400V_SHUNT_SC_FAULT_ID") and Shall raise after the fault response time of 100ms.
requirements.process:
1. Run the Diagnostics for every 10msec.
2. Provide high side voltage at 490V (input to vicor)
3. Provide Short to High failure at 400V side current shunt.
4. If the condition is match raise the level 2 fault with Fault ID 51
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 Shall set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU Shall equal to 51 ("DIAGNOSTIC_400V_SHUNT_SC_FAULT_ID")

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against power module I2C communication is compromised for a calibratable time & HVDCDC not in normal state
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a Level 2 fault if the enabling of the power module is not successful after a calibratable time since the power module is commanded to be enabled.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x11(17) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_02_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 2500ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
requirements.process:
Process 1:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) is On.
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
3. Ensure that NHVDCDCFault is equal to 0x0 (no fault) on the CAN trace window.
4. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and output section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x11(17) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_02_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 2500ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against fault if the enabling of the power module is not successful after a calibratable time since the power module is commanded to be
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a Level 2 fault if the enabling of the power module is not successful after a calibratable time since the power module is commanded to be enabled.
requirements.inputs:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) is On.
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
3. No fault condition shall be observed on the CAN trace window.
4. Disable the converter power module status
5. Keep the VICOR output voltage less than half of the VICOR input voltage minus 10V(VEnable_C)
6. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
7. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Enable" substate of the "HV_ON" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised minus 1500ms.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 3:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x0 (“No Fault")
At Step 6:
Below CAN messages are observed
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x11(17) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_02_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 2500ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
requirements.process: Verify that the HVDCDC application does not transit from the "Enable" substate to the “Precharge” substate after 1.5 seconds when the VICOR output voltage is less than half of the VICOR input voltage minus 5V(VEnable_C),if the power module status is set to disable and enters into the “Faulty” state.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x11(17) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_02_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 2500ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state upon a HVDCDC level 2 fault and Faulty state not able to shutdown the system.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to "Faulty_PowerDN_SafeState" state upon a HVDCDC level 2 fault and Faulty state not able to shutdown the system.
requirements.inputs:
1. The power module status should be disabled
2. The HVDCDC application should be in Standby mode
3. Once the HV DCDC application is in the "disable" substate of the "Discharge" state within 10 seconds, increase VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to greater than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V and ensure VICOR output voltage more than 175V.
4. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Disable" substate of the "Discharge" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 4:
Below CAN messages are observed,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x4 ("Disable_power_module")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x7 ( "Shutdown_DisablePM").
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x16(22) and is raised after the fault response time of 10000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process:
Verify that the HVDCDC application does transits to the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state from the “Disable” substate of the Discharge state when the below conditions are met,
1. VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 15V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than 175V(HVDCDC_CAL).
3. The power module status is Enabled.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x16(22) and is raised after the fault response time of 10000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to open the solid state switch on the 400V side DC link upon the power module status confirms to be disabled.
requirements.purpose: When the power module confirms disabled, the HVDCDC shall send command to open the Solid State Switch on the 400V side DC link.
requirements.inputs:
1. The power module status should be disabled
2. The HVDCDC application should be in Standby mode
3. Once the HV DCDC application is in the "Disable" substate of the "Discharge" state within 10 seconds, increase VICOR output voltage (VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to greater than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 3:
Below CAN messages are observed,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x4 ("Disable_power_module")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x7 ("Shutdown_DisablePM").
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x5 (“Discharge 400V”)
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is equal to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw")
requirements.process:
Verify that the HVDCDC application transits to the “Open_400V_SolidStateSw” substate of the Discharge state from the “Disable” substate when the below conditions are met,
1. VICOR output voltage (VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 15V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The VICOR output voltage (VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than 175V(HVDCDC_CAL).
3. The power module status is disabled.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x5 (“Discharge 400V”)
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is equal to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw")

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against report a faulty state of the Power Module by raising a Level 2 fault if the Fault Pin (FLT) is pulled low.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall report a faulty state of the Power Module by raising a Level 2 fault if the Fault Pin (FLT) is pulled low.
requirements.inputs:
1. The input voltage to Vicor module should be greater than 480V (To start vicor conversion)
2. HVDCDC should be in HVON state
2. Set High side status of VICOR by making respective GPIO enable.
2. Fault Pin should be pulled low
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages shall be observed
NHVDCDCNBMFLT @ HVDCDC_NBM2_PDU via PMBUS shall equal to 1.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2 fault") 1. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU signal should provide Fault ID for Fault Pin (FLT) is pulled low
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall raises a Level 2 fault if the VICOR reports Fault Pin (FLT) is pulled low.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and output section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCNBMFLT @ HVDCDC_NBM2_PDU via PMBUS shall equal to 1.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2 fault") 1. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU signal should provide Fault ID for Fault Pin (FLT) is pulled low

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against raise a Level 2 fault upon the detection of the 800Vind sensing circuit failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open).
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault upon the detection of the 800Vind sensing circuit failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open).
(800Vind corresponds to the sensor with CAN signal name: "VHVDCDC800VAnalog")
requirements.inputs:
1. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms.
2. Provide the changes required on power board for OC
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 71 (“DIAGNOSTIC_800V_VICOR_IND_OC_FAULT_ID”) and this fault is raised after 500ms
requirements.process: Verify that the HVDCDC raises a level 2 fault upon the detection of the 800Vind sensing circuit failure circuit by open circuit.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and output section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 71 (“DIAGNOSTIC_800V_VICOR_IND_OC_FAULT_ID”)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against the detection of the 800Vemi sensing circuit failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open).
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault upon the detection of the 800Vemi sensing circuit failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open).
(800Vemi corresponds to the sensor with CAN signal name: "VHVDCDC800V_emi")
requirements.inputs:
1. On power board short R355 with high.
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 39 ("DIAGNOSTIC_400V_EMI_OC_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 500ms.
requirements.process: The power module output of the 800V side voltage is not received or the communication with the power module is compromised because of the 800V DC link voltage(EMI) sensing circuit failure by circuit short to high, the HVDCDC application detects the failure and raises a level 2 fault.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 39
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against the detection of plausibility failure of the 800Vemi voltage sensor..
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault upon the detection of plausibility failure of the 800Vemi voltage sensor.
Sensors delays need to be filtered and debounced.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
3. Adjust the voltage of input and output as per specs
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 76 and this fault is raised after 500ms
requirements.process: The HVDCDC raises a level 2 fault upon the detection of plausibility failure of the 800Vemi voltage sensor.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against the power module 400V side voltage output is less than half of the 800V side input minus a calibratable threshold in HV On state.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the power module 400V side voltage output is less than half of the 800V side input minus a calibratable threshold in HV On state.
requirements.inputs:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) is On.
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 470V)
3. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface 100ms right after the 800V voltage stabilize at 480V
4. Make 400V side voltage output shall less than half of the high side voltage minus 15V.
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 63 and this fault is observed after 100ms
requirements.process: The application does not transit from substate “Open_precharge_Ctt” to “Normal" substrate when the status of the precharge contractor is confirmed to be closed and transit to "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState".
requirements.validation:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 63
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against the power module 400V side voltage output is less than half of the 800V side input minus a calibratable threshold in Precharge state and the Power Module is enabled.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the power module 400V side voltage output is less than half of the 800V side input minus a calibratable threshold in Precharge state and the Power Module is enabled.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module such a way that the 400V side (Low Side) voltage should be less than half of the 800V side input minus a calibratable threshold. (For ex. 800V side voltage is 500V then 400V side should be ((500V*0.5V)-15V) i.e. < 235V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface 100ms right after the 800V voltage stabilize.
3. Make 400V side voltage output shall less than half of the high side voltage minus 15V.
4. NHVDCDCStatus shall be set as 0x03 ("Precharge 400V") using the CAN interface
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU
requirements.process:
The HVDCDC application shall set the state as NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown = 0x2 “Emergency_FaultyShutdown when the below conditions are met,
1. Set the 400V side (low side) voltage of the power module to less than half of the high side voltage minus 15V
2. The power module status shall be Enabled.
3. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
4. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to <TBD> ("2:1 Ratio failure while precharge") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
requirements.validation:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to <TBD> ("2:1 Ratio failure while precharge") and is raised after the fault response calibratable time.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against 800V DC link current circuit failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open) and raise a level 2 fault.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the 800V DC link current circuit failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open) and raise a level 2 fault.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms.
2. Make necessary arrangement to provide Short to High.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 54 ("DIAGNOSTIC_800V_SHUNT_SC_FAULT_ID") and shall be raised after the fault response time of 100ms.
requirements.process: Verify that when the power module output of the 800V side current shall not be received or the communication with the power module shall be compromised, the HVDCDC application raises a level 2 fault upon the detection of the 800V DC link current sensor circuit failure by circuit short to high
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 54 ("DIAGNOSTIC_800V_SHUNT_SC_FAULT_ID")

requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the over temperature of Vicor Temp measuring point and raise a level 0 fault.
requirements.inputs: VICOR low side shall detect temperature more than above HVDCDC_CAL specified temp range (lower limit) in degree.
requirements.outputs:
1. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 ("Level 0")

3. TVHDCDC4 @ HVDCDC_Status3 shall greater than HVDCDC_CAL specified temp range in degree.

requirements.inputs: Give a temperature more 100 degrees (HVDCDC_CAL) to the controller IC TC397 for calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL.
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2 fault")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU should provide Fault ID for detected over temperature for configured calibratable time.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall detects the over temperature fault of the microcontroller and raise a level 2 fault if the temperature exceed the calibratable temp value and remained for calibratable time.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for reporting overtemperature warning for the power module
requirements.purpose: Raise the level 0 fault, If power module reports the over temperature warning.
requirements.inputs: Command response for the STATUS_TEMPERATURE (0x7D) from Power Module
requirements.outputs:
1.Raise a level 0 fault if the debounce time (HVDCDC_CAL) is overlapped.
2.Fault ID <TBD> to be raised.
3.Set the DTC
requirements.process:
1.The standard PMBus command STATUS_TEMPERATURE (0x7D) shall be read and checked across the value 0x40
2.Increment the debounce counter
3.Check if the debounce counter maximum physical value is exceeded
4.The fault should be raised after a configurable debounce time is elapsed.
requirements.validation:
1.Verify that the application raises a Level 0 fault, in case of over temperature warning.
2.Verify the fault
3.Verify the DTC
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to raise level 2 fault if HVDCDC detects all 16 channels of ADC failed to read input.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise level 2 fault if ADC shall failed to get input or read input on all 16 channels.
requirements.inputs:
1. All ADC channel not getting proper input
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU with respective Fault ID
requirements.process:
The HVDCDC application shall set the state as NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown = 0x2 “Emergency_FaultyShutdown when the below conditions are met,
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. The power module status shall be Enabled.
3. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
4. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU with respective Fault ID
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU with respective Fault ID
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement against detection of power supply failure condition of the HVIL detection circuit
requirements.purpose: The power supply failure of the HVIL detection circuit shall be detected and report by HVHVDCDC
requirements.inputs:
1. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms.
2. Provide the HVIL power supply failure by removing 6V power from the LV connector.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages shall be observed on the MCAN interface
1. NHVDCDCHVIL @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 2.
2. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU signal should provide Fault ID for detected power supply failure of the HVIL detection circuit
requirements.process:
1. The HVDCDC shall reports the HVIL status as 2 when power supply failure of the HVIL detection circuit.
2. Verify that the HVDCDC raises a level 0 fault (NHVDCDCFault = 0x1) upon the detection of the HVIL detection circuit for power supply failure.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
1. NHVDCDCHVIL @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 2.
2. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU signal should provide Fault ID for detected power supply failure of the HVIL detection circuit
3. The HVDCDC shall raises a level 0 fault (NHVDCDCFault = 0x1)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

1. NHVDCDCHVIL @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 2.
2. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU signal should provide Fault ID for detected power supply failure of the HVIL detection circuit

2. Verify that the HVDCDC raises a level 0 fault (NHVDCDCFault = 0x1) upon the detection of the HVIL detection circuit for power supply failure.

requirements.logical_block: Response requirement against precharge takes longer than a calibratable time to complete.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the precharge takes longer than a calibratable time to complete.
requirements.inputs:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) shall On.
2. The 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module shall set to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V).
3. Ensure that NHVDCDCFault shall equal to 0x0 (no fault) on the CAN trace window.
4. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
5. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Enable" substate of the "HV_ON" state within 1.5 seconds, keep the low side EMI voltage (VHVDCDC400V_emi) less than half of the high side voltage(VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 10V(VPrecharge_C).
6. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Close_precharge_Ctt" substate of the "HV_ON" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 3:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x0 (“No Fault")
At Step 5:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 0x13(19) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_04_FAULT_IDt") and shall be raised after the fault response time of 2000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
requirements.process: The application shall not transit from substate “Close_precharge_Ctt” to “Close_400V_SolidStateSw” state when low side voltage (VHVDCDC400V_emi) shall less than half of high side voltage(VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) minus 10V(VPrecharge_C) and it transit to "Faulty" state.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 0x13(19) ("DIAGNOSTIC_SM_HVON_TIMEOUT_04_FAULT_IDt")
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Response requirement against 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to open/close.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to close or to open.
requirements.inputs:
1. Provide short to high on Power board .
2. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with signal value as 0x00 ("Standby") via Canalyzer to HVDCDC application.
3. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable" substate of the "discharge" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V.
4. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Open_400V_SolidStateSw" substate of the "Discharge" state to the time when the level 2 fault shall be raised.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 4:
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x5 ("Discharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw").
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts is set to 1("Close).
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x17(23) and is raised after the fault response time of 200ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application shall transits to the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state from the “Open_400V_SolidStateSw” substate of the Discharge state when the solid state switch status confirms to be Closed.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 0x17(23) and shall be raised after the fault response time of 200ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug shall set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")

requirements.logical_block: Fault requirement against 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to open/close or is stuck.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to close or the 400V side solid state switch is stuck open.
requirements.inputs:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) shall On.
2. The 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module shall set to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
3. Ensure that NHVDCDCFault shall equal to 0x0 (no fault) on the CAN trace window
4. Make necessary changes on power board to make solid state switch stuck open
5 Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
6. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Close_400V_SolidStateSw" substate of the "HV_ON" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 3:
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x3 ("Precharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug shall set to 0x4 (""PrechargeLS_CloseSolidStateSwt")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 79 and shall be raised after the fault response time of 1000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
At Step 4:
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts shall set to 0("Open).
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application shall transits to the "Faulty_PowerDn_SafeState" state from the “Open_400V_SolidStateSw” substate of the Discharge state when the solid state switch status confirms to be Closed.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug shall set to 0x4 (""PrechargeLS_CloseSolidStateSwt")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 79 and shall be raised after the fault response time of 1000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts shall set to 0("Open).

HVDCDC shall maintain the number of times the open load fault is triggered

Purpose1 | Level 2 fault shall be raised by HVDCDC if the 400V side solid state switch / eFuse is opened due to a fault condition.
Derived Requirement | No Such derived requirement is applicable.
Requirement Priority | High
Access Restrictions | No Specific access restrictions is applicable.

Output(s) | HVDCDC shall detect level 2 fault for 400V side Solid state switch / Efuse is opened.
HVDCDC shall set DTC for 400V side Solid state switch / Efuse is opened.
Process | Verify that the HVDCDC shall set DTC for 400V side solid state switch opened when Efuse shall opened in normal/standby state due to Fault condition
.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement of detection for temperature sensor Vicor_ind Temp (THVDCDC1, analogue isolator) failure.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the temperature sensor Vicor_ind Temp (THVDCDC1, analogue isolator) failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open) and raise a level 0 fault.
requirements.inputs: 1. The 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module should greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
requirements.outputs:
1. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x6 ("Faulty")
2. NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 71 ("DIAGNOSTIC_800V_VICOR_IND_OC_FAULT_ID")
requirements.process: Verify that the HVDCDC shall raises a level 2 fault upon the detection of the temperature sensor Vicor_ind Temp failure circuit by an open circuit.
requirements.validation:
1. Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 71 ("DIAGNOSTIC_800V_VICOR_IND_OC_FAULT_ID")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement of detection for temperature sensor 400V EMI Temp (THVDCDC2) failure.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the temperature sensor 400V EMI Temp (THVDCDC2) failure (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground or circuit open) and raise a level 0 fault.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
3. Make necessary arrangement to provide detection of the temperature sensor 400V EMI Temp failure circuit by Open Circuit.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 86 (“DIAGNOSTIC_TEMP_EMI_400V_FAULT_ID”) and this fault is raised after 50ms
requirements.process: The HVDCDC raises a level 2 fault upon the detection of the temperature sensor 400V EMI Temp failure circuit by an open circuit.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 86 (“DIAGNOSTIC_TEMP_EMI_400V_FAULT_ID”)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement of inhibiting all diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period upon wakeup / re-wakeup
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall inhibit all diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period upon wakeup / re-wakeup.
requirements.inputs:
1. Keep VICOR high side voltage greater than 700V to raise a level 2 fault.
2. Turn ON KL30.
3. Check the inhibit time upon wakeup by checking the time from the time when CAN communication shall start to the time when level 2 fault is raised minus fault response time of respective level 2 fault.
4. Turn off KL15.
5. Turn ON KL15.
6. Check the inhibit time upon re-wakeup by checking the time from the time when CAN communication shall start after step 5 to the time when level 2 fault is raised minus fault response time of respective level 2 fault
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
At Input Step 3:
1) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 1(0x1)("800V OV fault") and this fault is received after 2 seconds including 500ms inhibit time and 500ms fault response time.
At Input Step 6:
2) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 1(0x1)("800V OV fault") and this fault is received after 2 seconds including 500ms inhibit time and 500ms fault response time.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application inhibits all non CAN diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period of 2 seconds upon wakeup and re-wakeup
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 1(0x1)("800V OV fault")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement of inhibiting all diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period upon wakeup / re-wakeup
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall inhibit all diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period upon wakeup / re-wakeup.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send CAN signal NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd message as 0x03 ("un-used command") via CAN interface to HVDCDC application to raise a level 2 fault.
2. Turn ON KL30.
3. Check the inhibit time upon wakeup by checking the time from the time when CAN communication is started to the time when level 2 fault is raised minus fault response time of respective level 2 fault.
4. Turn off KL15.
5. Turn ON KL15.
6. Check the inhibit time upon re-wakeup by checking the time from the time when CAN communication is started after step 5 to the time when level 2 fault is raised minus fault response time of respective level 2 fault
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
At Input Step 3:
1) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal 37(0x25)("Invalid command timeout fault") and this fault is received after 12.5 seconds including 12 seconds inhibit time and 500ms fault response time.
At Input Step 6:
2) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 37(0x25)("Invalid command timeout fault") and this fault is received after 12.5 seconds including 12 seconds inhibit time and 500ms fault response time.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application inhibits all non CAN diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period of 2 seconds upon wakeup and re-wakeup
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal 37(0x25)("Invalid command timeout fault")

metadata.source_section: Requirement of inhibiting all diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period upon wakeup / re-wakeup
metadata.evidence:
Term | Description
REQ ID | SWRS_DIAG_79
Customer Req ID | SDS-HVDCDC-187
Milestone | B-Sample/PF2.0
Purpose1 | The HVDCDC shall inhibit all diagnostics on the vehicle CAN communication for a calibratable period upon wakeup / re-wakeup.
Derived Requirement | No Such derived requirement is applicable.
Requirement Priority | Medium
Access Restrictions | No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
Input(s) | 1. Send CAN signal NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd message as 0x03 ("un-used command") via CAN interface to HVDCDC application to raise a level 2 fault.
2. Turn ON KL30.
3. Check the inhibit time upon wakeup by checking the time from the time when CAN communication is started to the time when level 2 fault is raised minus fault response time of respective level 2 fault.
4. Turn off KL15.
5. Turn ON KL15.
6. Check the inhibit time upon re-wakeup by checking the time from the time when CAN communication is started after step 5 to the time when level 2 fault is raised minus fault response time of respective level 2 fault
Output(s) | Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
At Input Step 3:
1) Below CAN messages are observed on the CAN interface
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCD

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to latch NHVDCDCFault = 0x1 "Level 0" for 5 seconds (HVDCDC_CAL) or until the fault trigger is no longer present, whichever is longer.
requirements.purpose: Once a level 0 fault is triggered, the HVDCDC shall latch NHVDCDCFault = 0x1 "Level 0" for 5 seconds (HVDCDC_CAL) or until the fault trigger is no longer present, whichever is longer.
requirements.inputs: Continuously monitor the temperature rise (TVHDCDC5 @ HVDCDC_Status3) and fault ID (NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU) of the CAN interface, using a heat gun apply heat to the local sensor in turn U402 till the temperature raises above 85 degrees and hold the heat gun for 15 seconds then take it away.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x1 ("Level 0")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x8 (SSS over-voltage)
TVHDCDC5 @ HVDCDC_Status3 is less than or equal to 50 degrees.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x0 ("No fault")
requirements.process: That once a level 0 fault is triggered, the HVDCDC latch NHVDCDCFault = 0x1 "Level 0" until the fault is present for more than 5 seconds.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x1 ("Level 0")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x8.
TVHDCDC5 @ HVDCDC_Status3 is less than or equal to 50 degrees.

requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for NHVDCDCFault Latch response on level 1 fault.
requirements.purpose: Once a level 1 fault Shall triggered, the HVDCDC shall latch NHVDCDCFault = 0x2 "Level 1" for 5 seconds (HVDCDC_CAL) or until the fault trigger Shall no longer present, whichever is longer.
requirements.inputs:
1. Continuously monitor the fault ID (NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU) on the CAN interface
2. Raise a level 1 fault and clear the raised fault after 3 seconds
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
CAN messages are observed for 5 seconds,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 Shall equal to 0x1 ("Level 1")
CAN messages are observed after 5 seconds,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 Shall equal to 0x0 ("No fault")
requirements.process: Once a level 1 fault shall triggered, the HVDCDC latch NHVDCDCFault = 0x2 "Level 1" for 5 seconds (HVDCDC_CAL) if the fault Shall got cleared within 5 seconds.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 Shall equal to 0x1 ("Level 1")
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 Shall equal to 0x0 ("No fault")

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to activate and deactivate the individual diagnostic.
requirements.purpose: The activation and deactivation of the individual diagnostic in the HVDCDC software implementation shall be configurable via calibration.
requirements.inputs: 1. List of Fault ID’s, DTC’s to be configured via calibration for activation and deactivation
requirements.outputs: The HVDCDC shall activate and deactivate the DTC’s and Faults based on the configuration set.
requirements.process:
1. Provided NM message
2. Open the CANape tool and deactivate the any fault/DTC (For. E.g 800V_OV)
3. Provided the overvoltage at 800v side to generate over voltage fault.
4. 800V_OV fault should not be raised
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool and CANape Tool.
Verify that HVDCDC shall activate and deactivate the DTC’s and Faults based on the configuration set.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to inhibit all the diagnostics during the wakeup process.
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall inhibit all the diagnostics during the wakeup process:
- before transitioning from "Sleep" state to "Standby" state; AND
- within HVDCDC_CAL sec since the HVDCDC transitions from "Sleep" state to "Standby" state
requirements.inputs:
1. Continuously monitor the fault ID (NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU) on the CAN interface
2. Raise any fault before the ECU entering standby state
3. Set the calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL to inhibit the diagnostics
4. Provide NM message to HVDCDC
requirements.outputs: The diagnostics shall be inhibit when HVDCDC is transitioning from Sleep to Standby state.
requirements.process:
1. Raise any fault before the ECU entering standby state
2. Set the calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL to inhibit the diagnostics
3. Provide NM message to HVDCDC
4. Observe that the diagnostics shall be inhibit when HVDCDC is transitioning from Sleep to Standby state.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
Observe that the diagnostics shall be inhibit when HVDCDC is transitioning from Sleep to Standby state.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to inhibit all the diagnostics during the Sleep process.
requirements.purpose:
The HVDCDC shall inhibit all the diagnostics during the sleep process:
- after the transition from "Standby" to "Sleep" begins; OR
- in the "Sleep" state.
requirements.inputs:
1. Continuously monitor the fault ID (NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU) on the CAN interface
2. Raise any fault before the ECU entering standby state
3. Set the calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL to inhibit the diagnostics
4. Stop sending NM message to HVDCDC
requirements.outputs: The diagnostics shall be inhibit when HVDCDC is transitioning from Standby to Sleep state or in Sleep State.
requirements.process:
1. Raise any fault before the ECU entering standby state
2. Set the calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL to inhibit the diagnostics
3. Stop sending NM message to HVDCDC
4. Observe that the diagnostics shall be inhibit when HVDCDC is transitioning from Standby to Sleep state or in Sleep State.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
Observe that the diagnostics shall be inhibit when HVDCDC is transitioning from Standby to Sleep state or in Sleep State

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for precharge circuit failure detection.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the failure of the precharge circuit (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground, or circuit open) and raise a level 2 fault.
requirements.inputs:
1. Provide the open circuit on the power board precharge circuit
2. Send "Enable" command(0x1) from EDSU.
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 58 ("DIAGNOSTIC_PRSCHRG_CKT_OPEN_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 50ms.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application shall detects the failure of the precharge circuit by an open circuit and raises a level 2 fault.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
1. NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
2. NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 58 ("DIAGNOSTIC_PRSCHRG_CKT_OPEN_FAULT_ID")
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

REQ ID | SWRS_DIAG_85
Customer Req ID | SDS-HVDCDC-226
Milestone | B-Sample/PF2.0
Purpose1 | The HVDCDC shall detect the failure of the precharge circuit (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground, or circuit open) and raise a level 2 fault.
Derived Requirement | No Such derived requirement is applicable.
Requirement Priority | Medium
Access Restrictions | No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
Input(s) | 1. Provide the Short to Ground on the power board precharge circuit.
2. Send "Enable" command(0x1) from EDSU.
Output(s) | Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 57 ("DIAGNOSTIC_PRSCHRG_SHORT_TO_GND_FAULT_ID") and is raised after the fault response time of 50ms.
Process | The HVDCDC application detects the failure of the precharge circuit by an open circuit and raises a level 2 fault.
Mandatory Fields | No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for detection of failure of the 400V side solid state switch circuit.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect the failure of the 400V side solid state switch circuit (power supply failure, circuit short to high, circuit short to ground, or circuit open) and raise a level 2 fault.
requirements.inputs:
1. On the power board remove R402.
2. Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface cyclically every 10ms.
requirements.outputs:
Application shall provide CAN messages status as below,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 67 (“DIAGNOSTIC_SSS_OPEN_CKT_FAULT_ID”) and this fault is raised after fault response time 500ms.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC application detects the failure of the 400V side solid state switch circuit by an open circuit and raises a level 2 fault.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 67 (“DIAGNOSTIC_SSS_OPEN_CKT_FAULT_ID”)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for fault response if 400V side solid state switch is stuck closed or stuck open.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch is stuck closed or stuck open.
requirements.inputs:
1. KL30 and KL15(IGN) is On.
2. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
3. Ensure that NHVDCDCFault shall equal to 0x0 (no fault) on the CAN trace window
4. Provide Short to GND on 400V side solid state switch of the power board.
5 Send NHVDCDCCommand as 0x01 ("Enable") using the CAN interface.
6. Check fault response time from the time when the application enters into the "Close_400V_SolidStateSw" substate of the "HV_ON" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages shall be observed sequentially
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x3 ("Precharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x4 (""PrechargeLS_CloseSolidStateSwt")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 79 and is raised after the fault response time of 1000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x6 ("Faulty")
At Step 4:
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts shall set to 0("Open).
requirements.process: HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch takes longer than a calibratable time to close or the 400V side solid state switch is stuck open.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for fault response if 400V side solid state switch is stuck closed or stuck open.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch is stuck closed or stuck open.
requirements.inputs:
1. Provide Short to High on 400V side solid state switch of the power board.
2. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with the signal 0x00 ("Standby") via Canalyzer to the HVDCDC application.
3. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable" substate of the "Discharge" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V.
4. Check fault response time from the time when the status of "HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts" shall 1 to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed sequentially,
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x5 ("Discharge 400V")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0xA ("Shutdown_OpenSolidStateSw").
HVDCDC_SSW_pin_sts @ HVDCDCPin_sts shall set to 1("Close).
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x3 (“level 2").
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 80 and is raised after the fault response time of 1000ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug shall set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault if the 400V side solid state switch is stuck closed.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for detetction of 400V active discharge is not effective
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect if the 400V active discharge is not effective and raise a level 0 fault.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send CAN command NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd with signal value as 0x02 ("Safe State") via Canalyzer to HVDCDC application.
2. Once the HV DCDC application shall in the "Disable_3" substate of the "Faulty" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V and give more than 45V to 400V bus(400V EMI)
3. Check the fault response time by checking the HVDCDC applications transition time from "Active_Discharge_400V" to "Disable_400V_AD" substate of the "Discharge" state.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 3:
Below CAN messages are observed sequentially,
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 70 and is raised after the fault response time of 1900ms.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall detects the 400V active discharge shall not effective and raise a level 0 fault.
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to implement a watchdog monitor.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC Shall implement a Watchdog monitor
requirements.inputs:
1. The HVDCDC shall configure an internal watchdog.
2.The watchdog shall be programmed to trigger a reset or take some corrective action.
3. Provide the fault conditions so that watchdog can trigger HVDCDC reset
requirements.outputs:
1.The Internal watchdog timer shall monitor the proper functioning of the HVDCDC
2. If the HVDCDC fails to respond within a specified timeframe the watchdog shall trigger a reset
requirements.process: Verify that the internal watchdog is functioning properly by following input and output methods
requirements.validation: Verify that the internal watchdog is functioning properly by following input and output methods

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to perform Power On Safe Test for checking the safety of the system.
requirements.purpose: After IGN ON is detected, the HVDCDC shall perform Power On Safe Test for checking the safety of the system before start of operation
requirements.inputs:
Provide the NM message over CAN.
OR
The IGN ON shall be provided to HVDCDC.
requirements.outputs:
1. The HVDCDC shall perform Power On Safe Test which includes below test,
- Verifying that the HVDCDC shall receive proper voltage at each terminal of power board as well as controller board
- Verify the individual Communication modules like SPI, I2C, UART, CAN etc shall operational
- Verify that the Temperature Sensors and current circuitry shall function properly
- Verifying the integrity of stored data in non-volatile memory used by the ECU for storing calibration parameters and diagnostic trouble codes.
requirements.process:
NM message’s shall be received over CAN.
The HVDCDC shall wakeup from Sleep state.
The HVDCDC shall start performing power on self test.
Verifying that the HVDCDC shall receive proper voltage at each terminal of power board as well as controller board.
Verify the individual Communication modules like SPI, I2C, UART, CAN etc shall operational.
Verify that the Temperature Sensors and current circuitry shall function properly.
Verifying the integrity of stored data in non-volatile memory used by the ECU for storing calibration parameters and diagnostic trouble codes.
requirements.validation: Verify that the HVDCDC shall perform Power On Safe Test for checking the safety of the system before start of operation.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.outputs: Upon reading the response of UDS service 0x22F102, the response shall be 0x0000000000000
requirements.process:

2. Send the UDS service request(0x22F102) for reading the original VIN.
3. Read original VIN from NVM

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to implement the specific requirements for the DCDC stated in [SDS18-DIA01-01_DCDC].
requirements.purpose: Write 13 Byte current VIN into NVM upon UDS request (0x22F190) received.
requirements.inputs:
1.Provide 13 Byte VIN (current VIN) with the UDS service.
2. System is in Extended Session for reading the Security level 1
3. System is in programming Session for reading the Security level 2
4.UDS Request (0x22F190) service for writing current VIN.
requirements.outputs: Upon writing the UDS (0x22F190) service, we shall receive a positive response
requirements.process:
1. Send the UDS service request (0x22F190) for writing the current VIN with security level 1 access and security level 2 access.
2. Store the current VIN in NVM
requirements.validation: Verify that the current VIN is correctly written in the NVM, a positive response shall be recorded after writing the UDS service.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.

metadata.evidence:
Term | Description
REQ ID | SWRS_PMCID_03
Customer Req ID | SDS-HVDCDC-129
Milestone | PF2.1
Purpose1 | The converter power module shall be enable if the conditions are met to enable it.
Derived Requirement | No Such derived requirement is applicable.
Access Restrictions | No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
Input(s) | EDSU command NHVDCDCCommand == 0x1 "Enable"
The HVDCDC converter shall set the HVDCDC_CAL time
Output(s) | Enabling HVDCDC power module.
NHVDCDCStatus = 0x2 Enable power module.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x2
Enable the converter power module
NHVDCDCStatus = 0x2 Enable power module
NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x2 Enable power module
Process | The HVDCDC shall enable the converter power module if the following conditions are checked and fulfilled:
- The 800V side DC link voltage is greater than 480V (HVDCDC_CAL);
- The EDSU command NHVDCDCCommand == 0x1 "Enable" is received;
- The converter power module is initialised and the communication is established;
- No level 2 fault present.
Mandatory Fields | No such mandatory fields is applicable.
Pre-Loaded Values | NHVDCDCStatus = 0, NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0, NHVDCDCFault = 0.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to enable the converter power module (PF1.0).
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall disable the converter power module.
requirements.inputs: NHVDCDCStatus = 0x4, NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7
requirements.outputs:
Disable_power_module.
Disable the converter power module
NHVDCDCStatus = 0x4 Disable_power_module
NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7 Disable_power_module
requirements.process:
Upon the EDSU's request to shut down the DCDC while the HVDCDC is in "Normal" state, if the HVDCDC detects that the 800V side DC Link is being discharged (detection of main ESS contactors open), the HVDCDC shall disable the converter power module.
The HVDCDC shall transit to "Disable" state.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatus = 0x4 Disable_power_module.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7 Disable_power_module.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCStatus = 0x4 Disable_power_module
NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7 Disable_power_module
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to shut down the DCDC while the HVDCDC is in "Normal" state.
requirements.purpose:
Upon the EDSU's request to shutdown the DCDC while the HVDCDC is in "Normal" state, the HVDCDC shall disable the converter power module.
The HVDCDC shall transit to "Disable" state. The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatus=0x4"Disable_power_module".
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7 "Shutdown_DisablePM".
requirements.inputs: NHVDCDCStatus = 0x4, NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7
requirements.outputs:
Disable_power_module.
Disable the converter power module

NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7 Disable_power_module

The HVDCDC shall transit to "Disable" state. The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatus = 0x4 Disable_power_module.
The HVDCDC shall set NHVDCDCStatusDebug = 0x7 Shutdown_DisablePM.

Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to raise a Level 0 fault.
requirements.purpose:
If the HVDCDC is in normal (buck/boost) operation with HV power applied and it detects the power module I2C communication is compromised for a calibratable time, the HVDCDC shall raise a Level 0 fault.
.
The HVDCDC shall latch the last valid values received on these messages/signals until the communication integrity is recovered.

requirements.outputs:

Set DTC
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall latch the last valid values received on these messages/signals until the communication integrity is recovered.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault = 0x01

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to raise a level 2 fault and command to actively discharge the power module.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault and command to actively discharge the power module 400V side when it detects the 400V side DC link or output voltage is higher than half of the 800V side DC link voltage AND the power module is disabled AND the 800V side is under load.
requirements.inputs:
1. Set the 800V side (high side) voltage of the power module to greater than 480V(e.g. 540V)
2. The power module status should be disabled
3. The HVDCDC application should be in Standby mode
requirements.outputs:
Below CAN messages are observed,
1. NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x4 ("Disable_power_module")
2. NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x7 ("Shutdown_DisablePM").
3. NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown == 0x2 "Emergency_FaultyShutdown"
4. The level 2 fault should be raised in All States except Sleep state and Power down Safe state
requirements.process:
Verify that The HVDCDC shall raise a level 2 fault and discharge the power module when the below conditions are met,
1. VICOR output voltage (VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) is more than half of the high side voltage (VHVDCDC800V_VICOR) plus 10V(HVDCDC_CAL)
2. The power module status is disabled.
3. NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x4 ("Disable_power_module")
4. NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x7 ("Shutdown_DisablePM").
5. NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown == 0x2 "Emergency_FaultyShutdown"
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x4 ("Disable_power_module")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x7 ("Shutdown_DisablePM").
NHVDCDCFaultyShutdown == 0x2 "Emergency_FaultyShutdown"

requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
