requirements.logical_block: Requirement to turn ON and turn OFF the Power board 5VV supply via PMIC control.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall be able to turn ON and turn OFF the Power board 5VV supply via PMIC control.
requirements.inputs:
1. Send CAN signal NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd message as 0x02 ("Safe State") via CAN interface to HVDCDC application.
2. Once the HV DCDC application is in the "Disable_3" substate of the "Faulty" state within 10 seconds, decrease VICOR output voltage(VHVDCDC400V_VICOR) to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V.
3. Check the fault response time by checking the HVDCDC applications transition time from the "Disable_400V_AD_3" substate of the "Discharge" state to the time when the level 2 fault is raised.
requirements.outputs:
At Step 4:
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x6 (“Faulty")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is equal to 0xD ("Faulty_Disable400VAD")
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 (“level 2")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU is equal to 0x23(35) after the fault response time of 200ms.
NHVDCDCStatus @ HVDCDC_Status1 is set to 0x7 ("PowerDown_Safe_State_NHVDCDCStatus_NW")
NHVDCDCStatusDebug @ HVDCDC_Debug is set to 0x11 ("PowerDown_Safe_State")
The signals IHVDCDC400V @ HVDCDC_Status2, IHVDCDC800V @ HVDCDC_Status2, VHVDCDC400V @ HVDCDC_Status2 and VHVDCDC800V @ HVDCDC_Status2 shows last received value from the power board, values are not getting updated.
requirements.process: ON power board connect TP412 to 3.3V
requirements.validation: Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to support the Network DTC’s according to the MSL.01.04.0047 Diagnostic Network ID.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall support the Network DTC’s according to the MSL.01.04.0047 Diagnostic Network ID Design Guide.
requirements.inputs:
- Provide the CAN Communication Timeout fault by taking HVDCDC into bus-off conditions or excessive errors on the CAN bus so that acknowledge does not receive.
- Provide invalid data or missing message byte/bit which results in incorrect CRC. This will generate Invalid Safety Data check DTC.
- <Need to add for Invalid Safety check>
- Provide incorrect data length in message with respect to number of bytes transmitted over the message frame.
-The HVDCDC shall use the Network DTC’s as per MSL.01.04.0047 design guide.
requirements.outputs:
-The HVDCDC shall raise below network DTC’s coming from DCU ZC1
1. CAN Communication Timeout (U180087)
2. Invalid Safety Data Check (U180183)
3. Invalid Safety Counter Check (U180182)
4. Invalid Data Length (U18018F)
requirements.process:
HVDCDC shall list all the network DTC’s to be sent to primary controller.
The HVDCDC shall detect the network DTC’s mentioned in output
The HVDCDC shall report all network DTC’s mentioned in output
Store all the Network DTC’s into NVM
requirements.validation:
Verify that the Network DTC’s shall be reported by HVDCDC to the Primary controller shall be as per the range P1E00xx to P1EFFxx.
Verify that the HVDCDC shall Send DTC over CAN.
Verify that the Status should return after successful store in NVM.
Verify that the DTC’s store in NVM after power on reset.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement for monitoring the battery short circuit.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall implement to monitor fault and provide DTC for the short circuit to high failure to primary controller.
requirements.inputs:
Do the changes required on the power board to provide the Short to high failure.
Create a CAN frame format to provide the DTC information to primary controller.
requirements.outputs:
HVDCDC shall raise the Short to High Failure and the respective fault ID should be provided in NHVDCDCFaultID@ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU signal after the fault response time of HVDCDC_CAL.
HVDCDC shall continuously report Short to high DTC information for respective fault using <Add here > CAN frame to primary controller.
The DTC for respective short to high fault shall be in range as per SAE document.
requirements.process:
HVDCDC shall receive a Short to High fault in various (800V_EMI, 400V_EMI, 800V_VICOR_IND, 800V_VICOR, 400V_VICOR, 800V_SHUNT, 400V_SHUNT, Precharge, Discharge) sections/terminals of power module.
HVDCDC shall detect the Short to High fault in the respective sections/terminals of power module within the HVDCDC_CAL time.
HVDCDC shall send the Short to High fault DTC over CAN within the HVDCDC_CAL
HVDCDC shall continuously report the Short to High fault DTC to primary controller.
requirements.validation: Verify that HVDCDC shall continuously report the short to High fault DTC across respective sections/terminals of power module to primary controller.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields are applicable.

requirements.inputs:

requirements.process:
- HVDCDC shall receive a Voltage Sensor Stuck in various (800V_EMI, 400V_EMI, 800V_VICOR_IND, 800V_VICOR, 400V_VICOR, 800V_SHUNT, 400V_SHUNT,) sections/terminals of power module.
- HVDCDC shall detect the Voltage Sensor Stuck fault in the respective sections/terminals of power module within the HVDCDC_CAL time.
- HVDCDC shall send the Voltage Sensor Stuck fault DTC over CAN within the HVDCDC_CAL
- HVDCDC shall continuously report the Voltage Sensor Stuck fault DTC to primary controller.
requirements.validation: Verify that HVDCDC shall continuously report the Voltage Sensor Stuck fault DTC across respective sections/terminals of power module to primary controller.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to calculate and store the 4 bytes CVN (Calibration Verification Number).
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall calculate & Store the 4 Byte CVN (Calibration Verification Number) with in 120s of ECU power ON.
requirements.inputs:
Calibration Verification Number (CVN) shall be of 4 byte.
CVN shall be read from D-Flash on ECU power ON.
CVN timeout parameter of 120 sec
requirements.outputs:
Upon ECU startup, HVDCDC shall calculate the corresponding CVN value for the Calibration within 120sec of power on.
HVDCDC shall verify the calculated CVN with the CVN number stored in D-Flash.
- HVDCDC shall report CVN to primary controller.
requirements.process:
Provide power on cycle to HVDCDC
HVDCDC shall calculate the 4 Byte CVN with in 120sec
HVDCDC shall read the CVN stored in NVM
HVDCDC shall validate the 4 Byte CVN with Stored value of CVN
HVDCDC shall update the respective CVN parameter
- HVDCDC shall report CVN to primary controller
requirements.validation:
Verify that the time required for HVDCDC to calculate the corresponding CVN value for the Calibration is less than or equal to 120sec.
Verify that the 4-byte CVN shall be maintained by HVDCDC in the specified format.
Verify that the HVDCDC shall report verified CVN to primary controller continuously.

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to report the previously stored CVN when requested via diagnostics for up to 120 seconds.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall report the previously stored Calibration Verification Number (CVN) when requested via diagnostics for up to 120 seconds from ECU initialization until the CVN is recalculated.
requirements.inputs: Diagnostics request to read the store CVN value
requirements.outputs:
Transmit the previous stored value in “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU for 120 seconds after Power ON
Transmit the Calculated CVN value in “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU after 120 seconds
requirements.process:
Initialize the ECU.
Start the CVN_TX_TIMER for timeout
Check if CVN_TX_TIMER is less than or equal to 120s.
Read the CVN from Dflash.
HVDCDC shall send CVN value in “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU when request is received from diagnostic
Check if CVN_TX_TIMER is greater than 120s.
HVDCDC shall send calculated CVN value in “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU
requirements.validation: Verify that “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU is received on CANanalyzer within 120s from start up.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Verify that “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU is received on CANanalyzer within 120s from start up.

requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.

Read the CVN from Dflash.
HVDCDC shall send CVN value in “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU when request is received from diagnostic
Check if CVN_TX_TIMER is greater than 120s.
HVDCDC shall send calculated CVN value in “DCDC_CALIB_CVN” PDU
Mandatory Fields | No such mandatory fields are applicable.
Pre-Loaded Values | Pre-Loaded value is CVN stored in Dflash

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to configure as a secondary OBD II device.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall be configure as secondary OBD II device
requirements.inputs:
1. Provide the various faults like Short To Ground, Short To High, Open Circuit, Over Temp, Over current, Over Voltage, Under Voltage, Sensor Stuck, Implausible input to HVDCDC
2. Calibration ID and CVN shall be provided to HVDCDC
requirements.outputs:
1. The HVDCDC shall send DTC for each fault type (Short To Ground, Short To High, Open Circuit, Over Temp, Over current, Over Voltage, Under Voltage, Sensor Stuck, Implausible input) over vehicle CAN.
2. The HVDCDC shall store DTC for each fault into NVM
3. The HVDCDC shall continuously report all the diagnostics fault, CALID and CVN to primary controller
4. HVDCDC shall store Calibration ID and CVN into NVM
requirements.process:
1. Provide the fault (Short To Ground, Short To High, Open Circuit, Over Temp, Over current, Over Voltage, Under Voltage, Sensor Stuck, Implausible input) one by one on HVDCDC Hardware
2. HVDCDC shall detect the fault within the calibratable time
3. HVDCDC shall send the fault DTC over CAN within the calibratable time
4. The HVDCDC shall store DTC for each fault into NVM
5. provide the CALID and CVN to HVDCDC
6. HVDCDC shall store CALID and CVN into NVM
7. The HVDCDC shall continuously report all the diagnostics fault, CALID and CVN to primary controller
requirements.validation:
1. Verify that the HVDCDC shall continuously report all the diagnostics fault, CALID and CVN to primary controller
2. The HVDCDC shall send DTC for each fault type over vehicle CAN

requirements.logical_block: Requirement to report the equivalent failure codes onto the Debug CAN.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall monitor the Discharge contactor status against the command of closing the contactor, within calibratable time period and reset threshold. When the fault is set, Level 0 fault shall be raised
requirements.inputs:
1. The Signal NHVDCDCCommand @ EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd shall set with value as 0x00 ("Standby") via Canalyzer to HCDCDC application.
2. The HVDCDC application shall be set in the "Disable" substate of the "discharge" state within 10 seconds,
3. The VICOR output voltage(HVDCDCPwrModule400V) shall be decrease to less than half of VICOR input voltage(high side) minus 15V and give more than 60V to 400V bus(400V EMI).
3. The fault response time by checking the HVDCDC applications transition time from "Active_Discharge_400V" to "Disable_400V_AD" substate of the "Discharge" state.
requirements.outputs:
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 24 and is raised after the fault response time of 1800ms.
requirements.process: Verify that the HVDCDC detects the 400V active discharge is not effective and raise a level 0 fault.
requirements.validation:
Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and out section on CANalyzer Tool.
NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 shall equal to 0x1 (“level 0")
NHVDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall equal to 24 and is raised after the fault response time of 1800ms.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.

The HVDCDC shall perform E2E checksum and counter diagnostics on the following Tx PDU:
- NHVDCDCStatus1 shall be E2E protected.
requirements.inputs: The HVDCDC shall add CRC byte and frame count for the transmit PDU before sending
requirements.outputs:
The HVDCDC shall transmit TX PDU with CRC byte and frame count included.
The HVDCDC shall not transmit TX PDU if CRC byte and frame count is missing.
requirements.process: Verify that the CRC byte and frame count shall be included by HVDCDC as part of E2E protection in the TX PDU
requirements.validation: Verify that the CRC byte and frame count shall be included by HVDCDC as part of E2E protection in the TX PDU
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC software shall continuously monitor if it receives an invalid combination of requests for NHVDCDCCommand from the domain controller.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall continuously monitor if it receives an invalid combination of requests from the controller and raise a fault if it does, if the situation persists for more than a calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL
requirements.inputs:
The HVDCDC shall receive either of the below values,
1. NHVDCDCCommand signal with value 0x3 (Unused_NHVDCDCCommand_NW) OR
2.NHVDCDCCommand signal with value 0x4 (Unused1_NHVDCDCCommand_NW) OR
3.NHVDCDCCommand signal with value 0x5 (Unused2_NHVDCDCCommand_NW) OR
4. NHVDCDCCommand signal with value 0x6 (Unused3_NHVDCDCCommand_NW)
requirements.outputs:
1.NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU should provide Fault ID 37 2.When the invalid value for NHVDCDCCommand are received for a configured calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL and the debounce time for this fault shall be calibratable time (Default- 50 msec).
3.Set the DTC “0x1E2500”
requirements.process:
1.The HVDCDC shall receive CAN NM message.
2. The HVDCDC shall receive signal NHVDCDCCommand with any value mentioned in the input section.
3.Run the diagnostic to validate input command value EDSU_HVDCDC_Cmd @ NHVDCDCCommand
4.If the invalid value is received increment the debounce counter
5.Check if the debounce counter maximum physical value is exceeded
6.The fault should be raised after a configurable debounce time(50 msec) is elapsed.
requirements.validation:
1.Verify the CAN messages and CAN signal mentioned in input and output section on CANalyzer Tool
2.NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU should provide Fault ID 37
3. Verify the DTC “0x1E2500”
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields are applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC software shall continuously monitor if it receives an invalid combination of requests for NHVDCDCCommand from the domain controller (2).
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall continuously monitor if it receives an invalid combination of requests from the controller and raise a fault if it does, if the situation persists for more than a calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL
requirements.inputs:
The HVDCDC shall receive the below value,
1. NHVDCDCCommand signal with value 0x7 (SNA_NHVDCDCCommand_NW)
requirements.outputs: The HVDCDC shall raise the respective SNA Fault .
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall check for the value of NHVDCDCCommand == 0x7, If found then the software should raise respective SNA Fault if it exceeds a HVDCDC_CAL time in msecs.
requirements.validation: Verify that the respective SNA Fault is raised in case of receiving the value 0x7 on NHVDCDCCommand signal.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC software shall monitor the overvoltage fault of the 3V3 rail and raise level 2 fault.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to the Faulty state (Level 2 fault) when the overvoltage condition occurs in 3V3 rail to avoid the damage of surrounding H/W components.
requirements.inputs: The analog sensor shall sense the 3V3 Rail that if it goes beyond the HVDCDC_CAL value.
requirements.outputs:
1.NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 11 2.When the analog sensor detects the voltage more than HVDCDC_CAL a fault shall be raised after a debounce time (Default- 100 msec).
3.Set the DTC ”0x1E0B00”
requirements.process:
1.The HVDCDC analog sensor shall sense the overvoltage.
2. The fault with level 2 shall be raised within a configurable debounce time(100 msec) ,with Fault ID 11
requirements.validation:
1.To verify that the HVDCDC shall sense the overvoltage fault and NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 11 after 100 msec.
2. Verify the DTC ”0x1E0B00”
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC software shall monitor the overcurrent fault of the 3V3 rail and raise level 2 fault.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to the Faulty state (Level 2 fault) when the overcurrent condition occurs in 3V3 rail to avoid the damage of surrounding H/W components.
requirements.inputs:
1. Read the 3V3 rail voltage value from ADC sensor
2. Threshold value for 3V3 Rail “HVDCDC_CAL” shall be a calibratable parameter
3.The debounce time shall be “500ms”
requirements.outputs:
1.NHVDCDCFault @  HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @  HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 97 2.When the analog sensor detects the voltage more than HVDCDC_CAL a fault shall be raised after a  debounce time  (Default- 500 msec).
3.Set the DTC ”0x1E6100”
requirements.process:
1.The ADC shall be powered up and communicating.
2.The HVDCDC shall sense the current if it is more than HVDCDC_CAL and raise a level 2 fault with Fault ID 97 after 500 msec.
requirements.validation:
1.To verify that the HVDCDC shall sense the overcurrent fault and NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 97 after 500 msec.
2. Verify the DTC ”0x1E6100”
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC software shall monitor the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors and raise a level 2 fault in case it fails.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to the Faulty state (Level 2 fault) the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors to avoid the damage of surrounding H/W components.
requirements.inputs:
1.The HVDCDC shall monitor the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors.
2. The HVDCDC shall be in Normal state of application.
3.Load shall be applied on any operating mode(buck/boost)
requirements.outputs: Monitor shall be inhibited for 800V current measurement within the range -3A to +3A for a defined calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL
requirements.process: The HVDCDC shall monitor the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors, if it goes beyond HVDCDC_CAL
requirements.validation:
1.To verify that the HVDCDC shall sense implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors
2. Monitor shall be inhibited for 800V current measurement within the range -3A to +3A for a defined calibratable time HVDCDC_CAL

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC software shall monitor the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors and raise a level 2 fault in case it fails.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall transit to the Faulty state (Level 2 fault) the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V current sensors to avoid the damage of surrounding H/W components.
requirements.inputs:
1.The HVDCDC shall monitor the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V
2.Application state shall be in Normal state
3. Load shall be applied on any operating mode(buck/boost) in such a way that 800V current measurement goes out of range
requirements.outputs:
1.Monitor shall be inhibited for 800V current measurement out of the range -3A to +3A
2.NHVDCDCFault @  HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @  HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 108
3.Set the DTC ” 0x1E6C00”
requirements.process:
1.The HVDCDC shall monitor the implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V if it goes beyond HVDCDC_CAL
2.If the debounce time (500ms) is crossed, the application shall raise NHVDCDCFault @  HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
NVHDCDCFaultID @  HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 108
requirements.validation:
1.To verify that the HVDCDC shall sense implausible measurement of the 400V and 800V and NHVDCDCFault @ HVDCDC_Status1 is equal to 0x3 ("Level 2")
2.NVHDCDCFaultID @ HVDCDC_Debug_PDU shall provide Fault ID 108 after 500 msec.
3.Verify the DTC ” 0x1E6C00”

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC SW architecture shall provide the flexibility to add on additional modulised application software components.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC SW architecture shall provide the flexibility to add on additional modulised application software components.
requirements.inputs:
1. List of all the Application software component that can be modulised.
2. SW architecture shall have provision to add modulised application software components
requirements.outputs:
1. Make sure all the listed application SW components be modulised.
2. SW architecture shall allow easy addition, removal, or modification of components without affecting the entire system
3. SW architecture shall reduce dependencies between modules by using interfaces and contracts instead of direct dependencies
requirements.process: The SW architecture should design such a way that it shall follow methods mentioned in input and output
requirements.validation: Verify that SW architecture shall follow methods mentioned in input and output
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Required resource usage limits : 60% of RAM, Flash, EEPROM resources used at PF3.0 level
requirements.purpose:
The following resource usage limits are desired:
<= 60% of RAM, Flash, EEPROM resources used at PF3.0 level
requirements.inputs:
1. On PF3.0 release SW, calculate the RAM, Flash and EEPROM resources used in full load condition
2. On PF3.0 release SW, calculate the RAM, Flash and EEPROM resources used in normal condition
requirements.outputs:
1. On PF3.0 release SW, the RAM, Flash and EEPROM resources used in full load condition shall be less than or equal to 60 percent.
2. On PF3.0 release SW, the RAM, Flash and EEPROM resources used in normal condition shall be less than or equal to 60 percent.
requirements.process: Verify that on PF3.0 release SW the RAM, Flash and EEPROM resources shall follow the conditions mentioned in input and output
requirements.validation: Verify that on PF3.0 release SW the RAM, Flash and EEPROM resources shall follow the conditions mentioned in input and output
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields are applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC shall implement unique calibratables to individual diagnostics such that all diagnostic functions can be enabled/disabled individually.
requirements.purpose: The application and calibration part numbers should be increased by one unit for each new release of the Software using the shared part numbers.
requirements.inputs: The HVDCDC shall use read previous 16 Byte value of application and calibration part number.
requirements.outputs: HVDCDC shall use the incremental 16-byte ASCII application and calibration value as the previous software release.
requirements.process:
HVDCDC shall set 16 Byte application and calibration value with default value.
HVDCDC shall increment last byte of application and calibration value with respect to previous release.
HVDCDC shall report incremental 16 Byte application and calibration value to primary controller.
requirements.validation: Verify that HVDCDC shall send the incremental 16 byte ASCII application and calibration value w.r.to the previous software release to primary controller continuously.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC shall implement a Trap/Exception handling to activate the trap.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall implement a Trap/Exception handling to activate the trap.
requirements.inputs:
1. Provide the trap condition by making CTRAP pin to low state (0 V).
2. Trap input shall be enabled. (TRPPEN =1), Trap state bit TRPS set to 1 (Trap state active)
requirements.outputs:
1. The HVDCDC SW shall perform Trap handling, It shall switch off the power devices if the trap input becomes activated.
2. The HVDCDC shall deactivate all the power outputs and set them into passive state.
requirements.process: Verify the Trap functionality shall be activated by the HVDCDC when the input conditions are met.
requirements.validation:
Verify the Trap functionality shall be activated by the HVDCDC when the input conditions are met.
Check the output on DMM or DSO for the particular Pin
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVDCDC shall implement a Trap/Exception handling to deactivate the trap.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall implement a Trap/Exception handling to deactivate the trap.
requirements.inputs:
1. Provide the trap condition by making CTRAP pin to low state (1 V).
2. Trap input shall be enabled. (TRPPEN =0), Trap state bit TRPS set to 0 (Trap state deactive)
requirements.outputs:
1. The HVDCDC SW shall perform Trap handling, It shall switch on the power devices if the trap input becomes deactivated (Provided that the IGN pin is ON).
2. The HVDCDC shall activate all the power outputs and set them into passive state.
requirements.process: Verify the Trap functionality shall be deactivated by the HVDCDC when the input conditions are met.
requirements.validation:
Verify the Trap functionality shall be deactivated by the HVDCDC when the input conditions are met.
Check the output on DMM or DSO for the Pin
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: The HVHVDCDC shall be compatible with the B-sample and C-sample of hardware using the same application.
requirements.purpose: The HVHVDCDC shall be compatible with the B-sample and C-sample of hardware using the same application. But different calibration must be used for activating/deactivating features and differences of the two hardware samples.
requirements.inputs:
1. Same Application Binary shall be provided for testing on B and C Sample HW.
2. Separate calibration hex file shall be provided for B and C Sample HW testing
requirements.outputs:
1. The same application Binary shall be worked on B and C Sample HW.
2. The features shall be activated /deactivated based on different Calibration file
requirements.process: Verify that the same application file shall be worked on B and C sample. Individual features can be enable or disable using Calibration file for both the HW samples
requirements.validation: Verify that the same application file shall be worked on B and C sample. Individual features can be enable or disable using Calibration file for both the HW samples
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Guidelines for configuring the unused pins of the Microcontroller for better EMI rejection.
requirements.purpose:
The unused pins of the Microcontroller shall be configured as per the below guidelines for better EMI rejection:
- Analog Unused pins Pad shall be disabled (PDISC register in FW is available to do so)
requirements.inputs:
1. Analog Unused pins shall be set to Hight state
2. Analog Unused pins pad shall be disabled using PDISC register
3. For more details refer Controller_pinmapper.xls
requirements.outputs: Observe that the Analog unused pins shall be disabled
requirements.process: Verify that the unused analog pins are properly disabled from software
requirements.validation: Verify that the unused analog pins are properly disabled from software
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria for this requirement should be as per SECTION-6 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
requirements.derived_requirement: No Such derived requirement is applicable.
requirements.access_restrictions: No Specific access restrictions is applicable.
requirements.mandatory_fields: No such mandatory fields is applicable.

requirements.logical_block: Guidelines for configuring the unused pins of the Microcontroller for better EMI rejection.
requirements.purpose:
The unused pins of the Microcontroller shall be configured as per the below guidelines for better EMI rejection:
- Digital Pins shall be configured as output and then Pulled Down from FW – (With Weakest Driver Mode)
requirements.inputs:
1. Digital Unused pins shall be set to Pull down/Pull down (Weakest driver mode) as per the better EMI rejection
2. Digital Unused pins shall be set to Input/Output as per the better EMI rejection
3. For more details refer Controller_pinmapper.xls
requirements.outputs: Observe that the Digital unused pins shall be disabled
requirements.process: Verify that the unused Digital pins are properly disabled from software
requirements.validation: Verify that the unused Digital pins are properly disabled from software

requirements.logical_block: Guidelines for configuring the unused pins of the Microcontroller for better EMI rejection.
requirements.purpose:
The unused pins of the Microcontroller shall be configured as per the below guidelines for better EMI rejection:
- Medium/ Low slew rate control can be applied to Connected pins as well e.g., SPI Pins for ADC communication with Power Board. This will reduce the sharp edges leading to low noise.

requirements.logical_block: Guidelines for configuring the unused pins of the Microcontroller for better EMI rejection.
requirements.purpose:
The unused pins of the Microcontroller shall be configured as per the below guidelines for better EMI rejection:
-The JTAG /DAP port shall be disabled / Pulled Low after the programming is done.
requirements.inputs:
1. JTAG /DAP port shall be set to Pulled down (Weakest driver mode) as per the better EMI rejection.
2. For more details refer Controller_pinmapper.xls
